Millions of us are denied protection under the law as the courts define away our rights and abandon the principles upon which our nation was founded. Millions of our children with disabilities are left behind as funding for the individual's with disabilities education act is denied year after year after year. Our much-beloved leader, Justin Dart, who galvanized Americans with disabilities challenged us when he said get involved with politics as if your life depended on it. Because it does. At no time has this been truer than now. There are choices. Jim crowe or of civil right, the politics of imprisonment or of liberation, the politics of division or of one America. One America is the America of opportunity, inclusion and independence. One America is embodied in the laws that the promise of disabled Americans their rights to live independently to pursue our happiness on our own terms. It is the promise our children make each morning when they say one nation, under God, indivisible, with liberty and justice for all. To my children, disability is just a normal part of the human experience. They have witnessed the most visible and rapid liberation of a people in our nation's history. They are proud Americans, but now they see that progress slowed and they don't understand. John Kerry and John Edwards will restore our progress and our hope for an end to bigotry. The hope for the pursuit of happiness for 54 million Americans. The hope for one America. We must give that hope back to our children. We must, we can and we will. A.d.a. , idea, holm stead, Mikasa take back the courts, voter can ree-Edwards.

speaker: Michael Negron
text: All elections are about the future. Yet my generation often opts out and sits on the side lines as politics make decisions affect our lives. But it's not that we're apathetic. 61% of our volunteer regularly. We paint school, work at soup kitchens and help communities across this nation. The problem is that many of us have lost faith in the political process. We don't see the connection between policies made in city halls an state capitols and what happens in our daily lives. What's most tragic about our political disenchantment is that our generation has the most to lose. Today, thousands of young soldiers are in Iraq, fighting a war sold under false pretenses. Out young soldiers are bearing the biggest bud of this war, yet this administration tried to cut combat pay for the troops while doling out tax breaks to the wealthy. At home, 1/3 of the nation's working poor are in our age group and we make up the majority of those without health insurance. College tuition costs are rising faster than our parent's income. It's getting harder and harder to believe in the American dream. And our faith won't be restored through volunteerism alone. Meanwhile, the current administration's blustering foreign policy risks alienating an entire generation of young people throughout the world. They're watching us and forming opinions of our country that they will hold for the rest of their lives. We must show them that we are a beacon of freedom and human rights around the world. Young Americans have clear incentives to become more involved in the political process. My generation will fight the wars to defend our country. With he will also bear the burden of debt racked up by this administration. America is the most powerful country in history and sit our generation's duty to make sure that this power is used for good at home and abroad. in the next few years, our leaders will make monumental decisions on war and peace, social security and health care. If we don't participate, if we sit on the sidelines, then we surrender any say over our own futures. But if we go to polls in November, if we make our voices heard, then our leaders will listen and our future will truly be our own.

speaker: Patty Judge
text: Thank you! Thank you. in the past two-year, nearly 82 million Americans lacked health care coverage. Last year, health care premiums rose four times faster than the wages of our workers. Skyrocketing health care costs not only hurt our families, they weaken our economy. They forced American businesses to pay higher premiums and cut the benefits of our workers. And they forced our seniors to decide between food and medicine. Democrats believe that all Americans deserve quality health care. It is a right and not a privilege. We can and we must fix the health care crisis now. Today, over eight million children lack adequate care. We will expand Medicaid and the children's health program so that never again will any child in our America go without medical care and seniors across this country should never again be forced to choose between meals and medicine. We will keep Medicare strong and we will never privatize it. We will also authorize the safe reimportation of drugs from other countries to make it easier for seniors to purchase the medicine they need. Elderly Americans and Americans with disabilities deserve to live in dillingnyty. Americans suffering from H.i.v. And AIDS and those who suffer from mental illness are our friends, our neighbors, our loved ones, they deserve to be treated with respect and care. Millions of minorities continue to live sicker and die younger than other Americans. We will fight to ensure that these Americans receive equal access to quality care. And as we fight to take care of our people, we will move forward with scientific and medical research. Americans deserve the best therapy treatments and cures. Under strict ethical guidelines, we will pursue STEM-cell research and offer hope to over one million Americans suffering from diseases such as alzheimer's disease, juvenile diabetes and parkinson's. Finally, Democrats will never turn our back on the veterans that served our country. No veteran should have to wait for care because of bureaucratic backlog. We in the Democratic party believe in an America where quality health care is available and affordable for all. Where every family can look to the future with hope and without the worry of rising health care costs. Thank you very much. Distinguished delegates, please welcome San Francisco attorney and former assistant attorney general Bill Lann Lee.

speaker: Phil Angelides
text: Thank you. Thank you. Thank you. I come here today from California, a place like America itself, of unparalleled potential. Every day my fellow Californians are inventing the 21st century. We stand poised to create the ideas, the jobs and the opportunities to secure America's future. And we are ready on November 2 to Elect A president, John Kerry, who embraces the values who have strengthened and built our nation. How did we build the shared prosperity that has been the envy of the world? We didn't just brag about our values, we acted on them. We invested our wealth to create opportunity for all. We created an economy of fairness, not privilege. We built the best schools, the finest colleges and the most prestigious research universities in the entire world. We protected the environment by saving our wilderness and our beaches. We lifted millions of our fellow citizens out of poverty. Together, with John Kerry and John Edwards, we will do it again. You see, in California -- the California idea is really the American idea, an idea as old as the republic. It reflects the values of my Greek immigrant family. Work hard, sacrifice, invest so the next generation can do better and everyone is given a chance to succeed. This idea is now threatened by debt, declining wages, and disappearing jobs. It is time to stand up for our values. It is time to Elect President Kerry. His lifetime of courage, commitment and public service tell us where he will lead our nation. John Kerry will open wide the doors of college opportunity. John Kerry will invest in the green wave of new energy technology, breaking the choke hold of foreign oil. And President Kerry will fight to strengthen America's families, cutting the cost of healthcare and investing in our public schools. Once again, we can be a nation of inspiration and aspiration. in the eyes of the world and in the hearts of our people. It now falls to us to engage in the simple act of democracy. Organizing on behalf of our passions and beliefs, to rekindle the unique spirit of America. Asking firsthand, then thousands and then millions to join us in electing John Kerry and John Edwards. Thank you. It is my honor to be here. Thank you so much.

speaker: Robert Menendez
text: My fellow Democrats, my fellow new jerseyans. And my fellow Americans. There is a great deal at stake in this election. But perhaps nothing more vital, more crucial, more fundamentalle to our future than America's providential role in the world. Today America is the only super power in the world. But we need a President Who understands the difference between a war of choice and a war of necessity. We need a President Who will guarantee a strong military and even stronger foreign diplomacy so we do not have to place America's sons and daughters alone in harm's way unless it's really in our national security interest to do so. We need a President Who will be truthful with the American people at home as well as with our allies around the globe. America cannot lead if others will not follow to be strong, to be a leader of the international community, to have friends who will stand by us when we need them. We need a President Who will engage the world, not isolate ourselves from it. And we need a President Who knows that the process of building alliances is not a sign of weakness but in fact it is a sign of strength, vision, leadership and purpose. You see, there are times when America may need to act alone. But you get a lot more firepower on your side when you can organize a posse. John Kerry has the ability and the credibility to internationalize our efforts in Iraq. And John Kerry will build a real coalition to win the wrart. And John Kerry has a plan to win the war on terror to make America safer and more secure, to make our country strong and respected arrange the sworled. -- around the world. Under John Kerry's plan, America will identify disrupt and eliminate terrorist networks any uniting the world around common interests, sharing good intelligence, drying up bank accounts, breaking up terror cells wherever we find them. John Kerry will appoint draketter of national intelligence so there is one individual with responsibility and accountability for intelligence operations and so that instead of investing failures we succeed in preventing attacks. John Kerry will reduce the stret of a nuclear attack by investing the resources needed to secure these materials worldwide. Imagine this, less weapons material has been secured in the two years after September 11 than in the two years before. John Kerry will make protecting America from this threat a national priority. John Kerry will finish the job in Afghanistan, a major an important front in the war on terror that was practically forgotten once we went into Iraq. John Kerry will expand our active duty forces by a 40,000 troops because he knows our overworked soldiers are now stretches too thin. John Kerry knows that when we must go to war, we must go with enough troops and enough planning to win the war on terror, secure the peace and earn the respect of the world. John Kerry will always remember that national security begins and ends with the soldier, sail ure and marine. Defending our freedom anywhere in the world. And let me tell you, when it is needed, John Kerry will not wait, he will not waiver, he will not hem or haw, he will not hesitate a single moment to use America's military force to capture and destroy terrorist groups and their leaders. To defend America and our friends and our interests, to protect this nation from danger. And John Kerry will not ignore our own neighborhood, latin America. The message John Kerry will send to latin America is different than the one latin America and latinos here in the United States have heard from President Bush. John Kerry understands, as those of us in the Hispanic community do, the importance of building bridges to these nations in our hemisphere so that our part of the world is safe, stable and prosperous. He will engage latin America with respect and with values that unite the americas behind common goals and against common threats. That's the Kerry plan. And if anyone doubts -- if anyone doubts his ability to accomplish these things, they don't know the John Kerry I know. The John Kerry who after leaving yale volunteered, the John Kerry who showed his strength of character around the bat tlefield, the John Kerry whose courage earned him a brongs star, silver star and three purple hearts, risking his life to safe others. That John Kerry, when Lieutenant Kerry discovered that he had a man overboard, he returned upriver to assist. The man in the water was receiving sniper fire from both banks. From an exposed position on the bow, his arm bleeding and in pain, in disregard of his personal safety, he pulled the man aboard. Crageourks strong, heroic, that's the President John Kerry will be. He will leave no one behind. And by his example he will lead America into a new era of security and strength and respect here at home and around the world. Now onto victory.

speaker: Roberta Achtenberg
text: I too, am a proud American. Nigs to being a senior Vice President of the San Francisco Chamber of Commerce, I am a lawyer, a mother, and a lesbian. I am proud to be all of those things and one thing more -- I am proud to be a Democrat. As a former assistant Secretary in the Clinton administration, I fought to ensure that our rights and civil liberties were kept safe. Safety without freedom is servitude and security without liberty is weakness. That is why this platform affirms that we will protect Americans and their liberty. in the words of justice steven, if this nations is to remain truth to the ideal symbolized by this flag, it must not wield the tools of tyrants, even to resist an assault by the forces of tyranny. This platform affirms our party's commitment to defend our civil liberties and uphold principles of equality. We will end the practice of racial and religious profiling. We will reform the patriot act to improve our terror-fighting capabilities and protect our freedoms. We also will protect the right of Americans to own fire arms, while keeping guns out of the hands of criminals, and terrorists. Democrats will appoint an attorney general who understands the difference between affirmative action and quotas. We will combat dispair rat treatment of women in the workplace. We are committed to the equal rights amendment. We will defend a woman's right to choose, consistent with Roe V. Wade. And we believe that abortion should be safe, legal, and rare. We Democrats call for gay and lesbian families to be fully included in the life of America. And we'll seek equal responsibilities, benefit, and protections for these families. We are committed to equal treatment of all service members and we reject the federal marriage amendment. Democrats embrace diversity to unite our people and strengthen our country and reject Republican efforts to exploits our differences and divide our nation. We believe in America's creed that out of many backgrounds and beliefs, Americans can unite as one. My parents came to America from Russia and Canada, because they believed that their children could live freely and equally under the law here. This platform commits us to those principles and that is why I am so proud to be a Democrat. Thank you. And now I'd like to introduce to you John Marx, the Mayor Of Tallahassee, Florida.

speaker: Rod O'connor
text: Good evening. On behalf of the dedicated leadership team it is my pleasure to welcome you to a Democratic convention that truly represents the strength and diversity of The Democratic Party. Exactly one year ago, we began planning the convention that doesn't just speak to Democrats, but brings new voices into our national conversation. It doesn't just take place in Boston, but takes place in America. It doesn't just do things the way we have always done them, but tries new approaches. For example, we have worked to make the voices of young people heard as never before. That's why we have announced a competition to give a primetime speaking slot to a college student. You'll hear from him within the hour. Here in the city that gave birth to political participation, this convention is opening new avenues for participation for a new generation. At a time when there are more choices than ever before to help you stay informed we have chosen to expand the pool of informants. Traditional news outlets are being join by blogger, talk radio toast hosts and cable television networks that have traditionally been overlooked. Recognizing that this is the largest gathering of political activist we have supported a training program so that people leave this convention more prepared to connect with voters than when they arrived. Boston is providing this convention with its home, its history and its nominee. But this is truly a national convention. Throughout the evening and other the course of the next three days, we are going to be connecting with satellite locations around the country bringing America to this convention and bringing this convention to America. Getting to this point has been a massive and memorable undertaking. It's involved over 35,000 participates coming to Boston. 15,000 members of the press. Some 14,000 volunteers, 4,000 miles of new cable in this building and the 43,000 delegates in front of me, all to build a convention that will nominate two people. John Kerry and John Edwards as the next President And Vice President of the United States. I'm excited about this convention. But like you, what I'm really excited about is where we are going to go from here. Because while I'm looking forward to a great convention, this is really about building a better America. And I look forward to seeing all of you again at the inaugural. Thank you.

speaker: Rosa DeLauro
text: Good evening. I'm Congresswoman Rosa DeLauro, chair of the Democratic platform drafting committee and a proud representative of the people of Connecticut. We gather this week in Boston with the same seriousness of purpose that gave rise to the sons of liberty to bring America together, to find its inner strength, and to set it on a new course. The country calls us as it called on those many patriots who were our forbears to build a good society and a better nation. That spirit infused the work of the 15 members of the drafting committee who set out not to cobble together a bunch of programs to suit a codery of interests but to produce a party platform that reflects our values and offers the vision of a stronger country. That determination took us from Portland to Baton Rouge to santa fe and finally to Miami for our final deliberations. We pursued our task with urgency because of the seriousness of the questions in front of us. Why was our country pursuing a foreign policy that leaves America isolated and less safe? Why a fiscal policy that presses off debt to the next generation? Why an economic policy indifferent to the export of our jobs and indifferent to the health care costs that crush our middle class. And why have the powerful been allowed to doggedly cut taxes for the most privileged knowing there was little left for the most vulnerable? We listen to experts of diverse perspectives, but we were also determined to hear from people living and working on the front lines. We heard from our firefighters and their families from port workers and from religious leaders. I could not forget army Lieutenant Paul Rye Croft there for the rescue at the world trade center among the first fighters in Iraq. His tour of duty extended three times. His testimony and others inspired us to get this document right. Today we present this platform to our convention and the country. And our vision of national security, we support our troops with the resources they need but we also commit to battle the full arsenal of our moral authority, our historic alliances and the power of our ideas and our values. This platform shows how we will be stewards for the environment. It presents the financial and moral incentives to produce a burst of job creation in America by saying that out of control health care costs are no longer permissible, we show that health care can be a right of all not a privilege for the few. Rather than the current cynical divisiveness we show how our floorous diversity can unite our country and once again make it a beacon for freedom in the world. With this platform in hand and the exuberant affirmation of this convention, John Kerry and John Edwards will lead a united party confident of its new direction and prepared to win the public support in November. Thank you and God bless you. It is now my pleasure to welcome the Governor Of Ohio who will introduce the national security portion of our platform, ladies and gentlemen, the honorable tom Vilsack.

speaker: Steny Hoyer
text: Thank you. Thank you. Thank you. Good evening, my fellow delegates. As we gather to nominate John Kerry for President of the United States, I am pleased to tell you that the Democratic caucus in the house of representatives is more unified than it has been in over four decades. That consensus was built on the basis of a positive agenda. Committed to enabling working Americans to realize their hopes and aspirations. To ensuring that America is safe at home and strong and respected throughout the world. To honoring our pledge to the greatest generation. Who defended freedom and returned to build the greatest economy the world had ever seen. To investing in our children who will be responsible for preserving and expanding America's promise. And to again restoring fiscal responsibility to our federal government. So that we may fulfill our commitment to both of those generations and generations yet to come. In just 99 days, America is going to Elect John Kerry, a son of this wonderful city, as the 44th President of the United States. And John Edwards of North Carolina as our Vice President. And on that same day, Democrats, Democrats are going to win majorities in the United States house of representatives and The United States Senate. And together, together we'll create a consensus for progress, responsibility, and community. That will lift our country and all its people to new levels of safety, security and success. Today, as all of you know, millions of families struggle just to keep the American dream in sight. They face the worst record of job creation since the great depression. And they are being squeezed by healthcare costs, college expenses, and gasoline prices. The Republican Party, my friends, has had its chance. Yet, is there any one of us that can say that America is better off today than it was four years ago? >> no! >> we Democrats are confident that we can and will lead this nation to achieve the full measure of its ... Greatness. Then and only then can we proclaim mission accomplished. With John Kerry as our commander in chief, we will protect the American people at home, confront our enemies abroad, promote freedom and democracy, and restore this great nation's good name. John Kerry's courage and strength were forged on a battlefield a half a world away. And in the great tradition of Democratic leaders who proceeded him, he is committed to a national security policy that sends a clear message to both friend and foe. United states of America will oppose every enemy of freedom and work with every friend of peace. We Democrats will also fight for fair economic policies that promote growth and extend opportunity to all our citizens. We will fight for fiscal responsibility and we will fight for a fair and simpler system of taxation. And let America know that we Democrats believed that morality morality -- morality demands that our generation not put its purchases on our children's tab. Social security's resources had endangered the retirement of our parents. We must reject the fiscal irresponsibility of this administration. Our Democratic party will continue to honor and promote the values that have steeled the American character. Inspired our national experience, and lifted us to new heights. Faith, family, hard work, personal responsibility and love of country. John Kerry and John Edwards learn those values growing up. And they lived them every day. And they -- they are the values that will guide us. 44 years ago, our 35th President Stirred our nation with the confident and optimistic assurance that together, together we could accomplish anything we set out to do. in the great inaugural address that the energy, that the faith and the devotion we bring to this endeavor will light our country and all who serve it and the glow from that fire he said could truly light the world. Today, today, my fellow Democrats, that fire burns anew. And it ill luol nates for all our citizens the principles that animate the soul of America. As Democrats have always done, John Kerry and Democratic majorities in the house and Senate will spark again the torch of America's greatness and America's goodness. We Democrats believe that America's best days are still ahead. Thank you, my fellow delegates.

speaker: Stephanie Tubbs Jones
text: Thank you, my fellow Democrats, my fellow ohioans, and the dynamic, dynamic Democrats from the 11th congressional district of Ohio. I want to thank you for the warm welcome. 28 years ago, on a night not unlike tonight, history was made when Barbara Jordan, the first African-American woman to represent Texas in the United States Congress, gave the keynote address at our party's convention. And it is because of the courage and hard work of people like Barbara Jordan and so many of you in this house tonight, that I too have had the opportunity to make history as the first African-American woman to serve on the ways and means committee of the United States Congress. And this week, everyone in this hall will make history when we nominate the next President of the United States, John Kerry. Although it has been almost three decades since Barbara Jordan's keynote address was delivered, the words that night still ring true today. We are a people trying not only to solve the problems of the present, but we are attempting, on a larger scale, to fulfill the promise of America. My friends, to fulfill the promise of America we must make America stronger at home and respected in the world. We must make the American dream a reality for all Americans. We must Elect John Kerry and John Edwards to lead the charge. Now, there are some who believe that the American dream is limited to those who can lobby for the special tax breaks, or limited to those who never sweat their growing health insurance bills, and limited to those who can afford expensive vacations overseas to visit the jobs that they sent there. But John Kerry and John Edwards have a different view. They believe in an America where the hopes and dreams of my parents, Andrew and Mary Tubbs, who punched the clock day in and day out as factory worker and the other as a sky authority cap, can bring -- as a skycap can bring them to the day where they could see their daughter serve as the hometown Congresswoman For Cleveland, Ohio. John Kerry and John Edwards believe in an America where the promise of America is open to all, no matter who you are or where you come from, as long as you are willing to work hard. Now, these are not Democratic values, these are not Republican values, these are American values. Sadly, these are values that this administration seems to have forgotten. On their watch, America has lost 1. 8 million jobs, and more than 205,000 of those jobs were lost in my hometown, Cleveland, Ohio. In Cleveland, the unemployment rate is over 11%, and more than 24,000 workers stand in the unemployment line rather than standing in the factory line. In our black and Hispanic communities the unemployment rate has entered into the double digits. Many of those out of work have exhausted their benefits, and the current administration refuses to extend them. And for many of those who have gotten jobs, they don't pay enough to lift their families out of poverty. I know I speak for millions of my neighbors when I say we are tired of seeing American jobs shipped overseas. We are sick and tired of working longer hours and finding it harder and harder to pay the mortgage, cover the costs of child care and save for college. We are tired of seeing our hard-earned tax dollars go to the haves and the have mores while the must haves, the could haves, the should haves, the maybes and the have nots have not at all. It is time, it is time for new leadership, it is time for a new vision. John Kerry and John Edwards believe that America can do better, and we can get America working again. In a Kerry-Edwards administration work will be valued, wor-administration work will be valued, workers will be respected and the businesses who invest here at home will be rewarded. John Kerry will jump start economic growth with a new jobs tax credit to encourage owners of small businesses like the small business my late husband, Mervin Jones built to create jobs now. Instead of lavishing giveaways on companies that ship job overseas, John Kerry will close those loopholes once and for all and cut taxes for companies to help them create jobs here at home. He will enforce trade laws so that there is a level playing field for our workers to compete on, and they will win, and he will always, and I say always, defend the right to organize. Let me tell you what oure tell you what our let me tell you what our country will look like after four years of a Kerry-Edwards administration. We'll have 10 million new jobs. We will have a deficit half the size it is now. Tax cuts to help those who are struggling to stay in the middle class and struggling to get their pay for things like health care and college tuition. And a minimum wage raised to seven dollars an hour. But more than that, we'll have an America that is stronger, more united, and more prepared to seize the promise of the future. In her speech so many years ago, Barbara Jordan observed that we are a people in a quandary about the present. We are a people in search of our future. And we are a people in search of a national community. My friends, the hard working families of this country deserve a President Who will work as hard as they do. Who will lead us to that future. Who will help us create that community. My fellow Americans, John Kerry is that leader. And with your help and hard work, he will be that president. Thank you very, very much.

speaker: Tammy Baldwin
text: My name is Tammy Baldwin and I probably represent the hard working people of south central Wisconsin. Almost six years ago these wisconsinites first elects me because we share a vision, -- affordable health care for every American. Only one candidate for President Shares that vision, and that is why we must Elect John Kerry. John Kerry believes as we do that health care is not a privilege for some but a right for every American. John Kerry knows that accessible health care is a cornerstone of a safe and strong nation. John Kerry will make affordable health care the priority that we need it to be. In Wisconsin and in every corner of country the American people are sending that message loud and clear. And we hear you. We hear Al biel of Wisconsin who wrote me to say -- "where I work, our insurance premiums have doubled. The insurance and drug companies are out of control. They are taking us to the cleaners. " well, Al, John Kerry won't sit back and watch while premiums rise four times faster than workers' wages. He has got a plan to cut down the premiums, cut out the waste and cut off the special interest groups that put corporate profits before patient care. We hear roy shores of Madison, Wisconsin, who stopped by my office to leave a copy of an insurance claim, showing a charge of $2,979.50 for a single injection. Talk about a shot in the arm! Roy, when John Kerry's president, he will put a stop to the abuses. Control spiraling health care costs and pass the savings on to American families. We hear aim of sun prairie, Wisconsin. Who told me her 67-year-old mother has to pay $300 to $400 every few months for her prescription drugs. Amy, as president, John Kerry will negotiate with drug companies, not for them. He will get us real discounts. We will add comprehensive prescription drug coverage to Medicare and he will eliminate loopholes that keep affordable medicines off the market. We hear emily bebe of Wisconsin whose husband runs a small auto repair shop. They are trying to afford health care for their family, including two young children as well as their workers. In one year the beebes paid their h.m.o. Almost $40,000 in premiums. She wrote -- "this is morning we typically earn in a year. It's getting to the point we have to choose between daily necessities and health care. " emily, John Kerry is a parent, too. And when he is president, we will cover every child in America period. And he understands the problems of small business owners, such as the beebes. So his plan offers help to pay for the cost of the coverage for them and their workers. John Kerry wants all Americans to be able to enjoy the same great health care that members of Congress and their families enjoy. And he will guarantee that right to family health care benefits for all of our families, including domestic partners. We live in an age of medical miracles. Perhaps the greatest of them all lies right in the heart of my district in the university of Wisconsin in Madison. It was there in 1998 that dr. Jamie Thompson and his team of researchers successfully established and sustained several lines of embryonic STEM cells in a laboratory. Scientists believe that these STEM cells may unlock the treatments and cures to our most debilitating illnesses and injuries. Alzheimer's, parkinson's, part lees, spinal cord injuries, diabetes, an enemy's list of ailments we could penlly element or ease. But in order to satisfy the far right wing of our party, our current President Is holding these STEM cells and all that they promised hostage. When John Kerry is elected president, will he waste no time in lifting the restrictions on step creme research. He will put our people's health over his political health, and that's the kind of President We deserve. The most sacred tenant of the physicians' oath is, first, do no harm. But every day Americans are facing great harm. Workers who can't afford their premiums. Small business owners who cannot provide benefits to their workers. Seniors who cannot afford their prescriptions. Families who can't see their doctor of choice. If our leaders can promise health care for all to Iraq, why can't we do the same here at home? We hear you, America. We say when we talk about health care, there's only one candidate for President Who listens, one candidate who hears us, one candidate who will make high quality health care affordable for all. So when we talk about health care, we must also talk about and do all that we can to Elect As the next President of the United States John Kerry. Thank you.

speaker: Terry McAuliffe
text: Thank you. Thank you. My fellow Democrats, stand up, stomp your feet, our March to victory begins tonight! George Bush -- George Bush promised that he would be a unitor, not a divider. Well, let me ask you, are the Democrats united? Audience: yes! >> are we energized? Audience: yes! >> are we ready to win? Audience: yes! >> four years ago when we gathered in Los Angeles, America was enjoying the greatest period of prosperity and peace that our nation has ever known.more and more Americans were working and seeing their paychecks rise. We had ended 30 years of budget deficits. And around the world, people looked to America as a beacon of hope. It had taken hard work and tough decisions to get America back on the right track, but we did it. Americans agreed from the west to the east, from the north to the south, Americans voted for Al Gore in record numbers. We want a half a million more votes than George Bush and Dick Cheney. If only we had gotten one more vote on The United States Supreme Court. Four years later, the results of this administration's policies are clear for all to see. Three million more Americans have slipped into poverty and four million more have no health insurance at all. We have the largest budget deficits in the history of America. And no administration since the great depression has a worse record when it comes to creating jobs. My fellow Democrats, I think it's fair to say we can do better. Because I'm here to tell you your Democratic party is in the best shape that it's ever been in. We are ready for this election. We are equipped. We are primed, and we are going to win. When I was honored to be elected Chairman Of this great party in 2001, I promised all of you that we would organize, energize and revitalize the Democratic party. And I'm here to report -- mission accomplished. We have forged a party for the 21st century. We have upgraded our technology and created a 160-million person data base. Every day more Americans are going to Democrats. Org to share their ideas and to help us spread the word. In our brand-new state-of-the-art national headquarters building was built with cash, not loans. That's what I call real fiscal responsibility in Washington. In our party, we're honoring America's beautiful diversity. A Hispanic outreach program, a new woman's vote center, and a new voting rights institute because every American counts, every vote counts, and we will never again allow a discrimination to occur at the ballot box. You have my word on that. And we've expanded our grass roots, training Sessions to bring young people back into our party, and millions more low-dollar donors with a younger average age than ever before. Today we have an energetic, forward-looking party. Today, my friends, we've built a new Democratic party up and down the ballot. A party ready for this election and ready for this new century, we're a party united and ready to lead. Now let me say now, and I want to personally thank the 10 great candidates who ran for President On the Democratic nomination this year. And I want to thank everyone who supported them. And I thank all of you for helping to unite this party around the common goal that we share -- to bring new leadership to The White House because this November, we're going to Elect John Kerry and John Edwards as the next President And Vice President of the United States of America. John Kerry is a real American hero. He volunteered to serve on the frontlines of Vietnam and risked his life to save the lives of the men under his command. And when he came home, he dedicated himself to giving a voice to those our government has forgotten -- veterans who aren't getting the health care that they've earned. Middle class families who ask only that their government live by their values and not the values of h.m.o. 's and Hal Burton. And John Edwards' story is the story of the American dream, the mill worker's son who worked hard to get a good education and devoted his life to standing up for those who needed a champion. A leader who offered new ideas on health care and battling terrorism. Together John Kerry and John Edwards will offer real leadership because they have a plan. A plan for new jobs, rising incomes, and greater opportunities for all. A plan to make health care more affordable for all Americans, and a plan to win the war on terror and restore America's standing in the world. My fellow Democrats, all this is possible with your passion, your energy, and your hard work. Because as of today, we only have 99 days to go. Less than 100 days until John Kerry and John Edwards give America the greatest administration this country has ever seen. Fellow Democrats, we will do this. The Republicans, they will throw everything they've got at us. They'll send hundreds of millions of dollars to blanket our air waves with weapons of mass deception. But it won't work. So for the next 99 days, work hard, knock on doors, call your neighbors, keep the faith, don't let up, don't back down, don't stop, don't rest. We've put people back in the shining center of our party. Now let's put people back in charge of America. Thank you very much.

speaker: Tom Menino
text: Thank you. Thank you. Fellow Democrats, welcome to Boston. A city -- a city with so much history and so much future. It's an honor for us to be your host. We are proud of our traditions here in Boston. One is the way we talk. Here are three things that sound great with a Boston accent. Fenway park. A lobster dinner by the harbor. An my favorite, I'm proud to be a member of The Democratic Party! Please join me in saying thank you to thousands of volunteers and workers who labored for so long to make this week in Boston possible. We all have to put up with some inconveniences because we cannot allow threats from our enemies to undermine our Democratic process. We gathered in city and state where America really began. Home of the pilgrims, and the first thanksgiving. America's first public school, Boston, the Boston public library putting knowledge and culture into the hands of people who work with their hands. America's industrial revolution was born and the telephone, the first mutual fund, it all got started here. The American revolution was ignited right outside these doors. Walk our freedom trail and trace the footsteps of the patriots who risked their lives to change the course of history. We know our cause, we know the stakes and this week we'll tell America, get ready, take heart, the next four years will be better than last. I am a Democrat because Democrats believe the future will be better than the past. We have to make that happen. Like those of you who share my roots, urban, ethnic neighborhood. My views were shaped as a youngster in Harry Truman's America. Our parents taught us to respect our neighbors and look out for them too. I dream -- our dream was the American dream, to grow up and join the ranks of the great American middle-class. The g.I. Bill resulted in the greatest expansion of the middle-class that America has ever known. That was a Democratic idea, and it will open the doors to opportunity and rewarded hard work. Democrats gave us that. America works best when it works for all. When Democrats are in charge, I see it every time. I walk into my hospitals or high-tech firms. I look at the faces of our workers and see the whole human race. It's that young person who makes us number one, a new economy. And more than 90 college and universities in Massachusetts draw young people from all over the world. Like any city, we've had our setbacks. We didn't always live up the our ideals. From our time in Boston when catholics were not welcomed, where no Irish need apply. Two generations ago, our streets were marred by racial strife. Some even said that Italian Americans would never be Mayor Of Boston. I took care of that 11 years ago. Yet, since then, we have found countless ways to bring people together, to build neighborhoods, revitalize main streets to give opportunities to young people and those who just arrive. We have differences, but we have always found ways to build bridges between them. And Boston has become a better city for it. So as mayor, I'm proud to welcome you to the new Boston, home of the new bostonians from every corner of the world. Boston is and always has been a gateway for immigrants to America. My own grandparents among them. Look at the faces here inside this hall. Look around America and see yourself. We are difference in many ways, yet no one is left out of our party. No one has told they can't sit down to a place at our table. Here in Boston, we know that America works best when America works for all. Mark my words, this election is the most crucial of my lifetime. All of us know America is not working as well as it should be. We deserve a President Who will be our partner and not a spectator. We need a White House that won't forget the little guy. Our nation will be safer. When we build respect for America around the world. We began working to bring you all here, we had no idea the convention would nominate one of our own. The primaries gave us a leader and has been tested in combat and not found wanting. He has faced many enemy fire and never flinched. When you leave this building thursday night, you'll be marching behind a genuine leader, a decorated war hero. Our senator, our neighbor, our next President of the United States, John Kerry. The past four years have been hard on America. Now it's time to set things right. To win it fair and square and it starts right here. This time, we have to work harder. There is too much at stake to let victory be stashed away -- to be snatched away again to be afraid of the power of united peep. There's too much at stake to let the entuesdayiasm of the young be squashed by cynics in the media. So when you leave our city, take your lessons that never die. At a certain point in history, there comes a time to set a new course. That time is now. Fortune favors the brave. America needs you and it starts with you tonight. Let's restore what's best about America. in the election of 2004, it is decided. Just remember it started right here, right here in Boston. Thank you, Democrats. Thank you America.

speaker: Tom Vilsack
text: Thank you. Thank you. Thank you. Thank you very much. Thank you. It is -- thank you. Thank you. Thank you. Thank you, thank you. Thank you very much. Thank you. It is my privilege to address you as the co-chair of the platform committee. This is the first national Democratic platform since the awful events much 9/11 and there is no more important function of government than to protect the safety and the security of its people. Our platform gives that critical task the priority it deserves. Protecting our security is a multifaceted challenge and we address the many paths we must pursue. These include stopping the spread of weapons of mass destruction, promoting democracy and freedom, strengthening our overstretched military forces and achieving real energy independence for our nation. All of these goals are essential. None of them can be ignored. As we take these important steps to protect our security and our values at home and around the world, we recognize that we are at war with an enemy that must be destroyed. The recent release of the 9/11 commission report has revealed that many deficiencies in the past anti-terror efforts but to achieve victory on the war on terror we must be strong, we must be innovative and we must be bold. Our platform describes how we can improve U.S.. Intelligence cutting off funding for terrorists and prevent nations from provide ago training ground for terrorist operations. Further, we commit to direct an immediate military action to capture or destroy terrorist groups or their leaders. In short, we will confront al qaeda and its terror affiliates around the globe with every tool available and we will do what this administration has failed to do, use diplomacy to create an effective worldwide coalition against terror. We must directly confront terrorist access to weapons of mass destruction, particularly nuclear arms and we are committed to leading an international effort to stop nuclear programs in North Korea, Iran, and elsewhere. We propose four steps to stop terrorists from acquiring nuclear weapons including a renewed effort to lock away or destroy existing weapons and materials, a critical area where this current administration has failed. Americans have come to understand that John Kerry and John Edwards have the experience and the fortitude to lead our country and the world in the war against terror. We will be sure that our people on the we will be sure that our people on the frontlines, our police, our firefighters, and other emergency personnel have the strange and the resources to do their critical work. The current administration has not adequately supported these heroes but we will. I want to thank you for the privilege of co-chairing the platform committee. I'm proud to recommend to you this 2004 platform which, like our nominees for President And Vice President Will make our country stronger at home and more respected in the world. Now, ladies and gentlemen, it is my pleasure to help you -- the former Secretary Of defense during the Clinton administration, the honorable William Perry.

speaker: William Perry
text: Thank you. Ladies and gentlemen, I thank you for the opportunity to address this convention. I have been involved in cold war crises when nuclear war was threatened. I've been involved in the development of stealth and smart weapons when deterrents was threatened. I have dealt with the dangerous turmoil in Russia and eastern europe after The Soviet Union disintegrated. But I have never -- I have never been as worried about the management of our country's security as I am today. It is time for a change. Let me tell you why I'm here to support John Kerry for president. John Kerry has a lifetime of experience and a deep knowledge of national security. To lead this country through the serious challenges that face our nation, he has a plan to combat terrorism more effectively, he has a plan to protect our homeland, he has a plan to turn the tide against proliferation of weapons of mass destruction, to eliminate nuclear terrorism and contain bioterrorism. He has a plan to get us on track in Iraq. John Kerry understands that we must keep our military the strongest in the world. in the heat of the last presidential campaign the Republicans promised our military that help is on the way. Instead, our forces have been dangerously overstretched. Against sound military advice, the administration based its planning on the belief that Iraqis would welcome U.S.. Forces as liberators. Our soldiers and our marines have had to bear the brunt of this stunning miscalculation. Based on his own service, John Kerry understands what our troops need. With John Kerry as president, help really will be on the way. And he understands that America must restore its international position of leadership. Without the strong support of our allies and partners, we simply cannot protect our security. We cannot rebuild Iraq alone. We cannot prevent nuclear proliferation alone. We cannot stop terrorism alone. We must isolate the terrorists not isolate The United States. John Kerry understands this and will lead our country and will lead the world toward peace. The stakes could not be higher. Thank you very much. And now it is my pleasure to introduce Congresswoman Desolis of Los Angeles to talk about homeland security and energy independence.

speaker: Adam Smith
text: My name is Adam smith. I represent the ninth district of Washington state, and I'm here today because I know we can be stronger at home and more respected in the world again. John Kerry can bring that vision back to The White House. Responsible leadership, real strength, opportunity for every American. I know the difference responsible leadership means to our country. Responsible leadership isn't a banner that says mission accomplished. It's the hard work of diplomacy that builds a coalition and knows that working with other countries isn't weakness, it's strength. It means honoring our veterans. I have seen John Kerry with his band of brothers who put their lives in his hands 35 years ago and I know that John Kerry understands real patriotism means keeping our promises to our veterans. Real strength means energy dependence. Washington state's legendary Senator Scoop Jackson and Warren Magnusen faced up to the cold war threat and John Kerry knows where today's real threats comes from. He will lead America to a cleaner and more secure energy-independent future. I know how important opportunity is. Like most of you, I went to public schools. I worked my way through college loading trucks for u.p.s. But couldn't have done it without federal student loans and grants. I was able to become the first in my family to graduate from college. My father would have been proud. He worked his whole life as a ramp servicemen at the airport. He believed in his local machinist union. I never fully knew what it all meant. Until I was 19 and my father died. It was only because of my father's union and the benefits he had worked a lifetime to secure that my family could continue to pay the bills so that I could finish my education. I know in my heart how much America needs a President Who will value families and invest in education. We have work to do. For John Kerry, for Washington state, for our country. For me and my wife Sarah, it's all about ensuring our children, carol and Jack, and all children have the future they deserve. This campaign, this cause, this fight, is our chance to give them the future, our families worked a lifetime to give us. It's our chance to make America stronger at home and respected in the world. And more importantly, it's our chance to make John Kerry the next President of the United States. Thank you very much. Thank you.

speaker: Allyson Schwartz
text: Fellow delegates, fellow pennsylvanians, Americans, many are saying that this election will be decided on the question of values. I hope it's true, because our values aren't defined by political analysts or campaign ads, or the media. Because values are more than just something we talk about. They're something we feel and we act on. They're the aspirations of a parent for a child, the concern for one neighbor for another. The determination of a nation to be safe, strong, and secure. The truest measure of our values is found in our actions and our priorities. An economy anchored in the belief that America works best when it works for all of us, public schools that recognize and help realize the potential of each and every child. A healthcare system focused on the well being of patients, not only on profits. And a foreign policy that will build on America's leadership in the world and that also respects a long-standing ally. Opportunity, responsibility, community, service, strength, respect, these are the values of our Democratic party and the values that John Kerry will bring to The White House. Our values are reflected in John Kerry's record of fighting for specific plans to restore our economy. We will end tax breaks that reward American companies who are shipping American jobs overseas. Our values are reflected in John Kerry's dedication to improving public education. We will insist on standards and accountability in our schools and hold ourselves accountable for helping American schools succeed. Our values are reflected in John Kerry's record of fighting to make healthcare more affordable and more available with prescription drug reforms that benefit seniors and patients, not c.e.o.'s and lobbyists. And our values are reflected in John Kerry's battle-tested vision for America's role in the world. It's time for our foreign policy to set America on a path to independence of foreign oil, one that respects our long-standing allies, and one that recognizes the need for America to be respected in the world. So, we are going to do more than talk about values. We are going to demonstrate American values in our priorities and in our actions. Together, with John Kerry as our president, we will make America strong at home and respected in the world. Thank you. And God bless you. Thank you.

speaker: Barack Obama
text: Thank you so much, thank you, thank you so much, thank you so much, thank you, thank you. Thank you Dick Durbin you make Us all proud. On behalf of the great state of Illinois, crossroads of a nation, land of Lincoln, let me express my deep gratitude for the privilege of addressing this convention. Tonight is a particular honor for me because, let's face it, my presence on this stage is pretty unlikely. My father was a foreign student, born and raised in a small village in Kenya. He grew up herding goats, went to school in a tin-roof shack. His father, my grandfather, was a cook, a domestic servant. But my grandfather had larger dreams for his son. Through hard work and perseverance my father got a scholarship to study in a magical place; America which stood as a beacon of freedom and opportunity to so many who had come before. While studying here, my father met my mother. She was born in a town on the other side of the world, in Kansas. Her father worked on oil rigs and farms through most of the depression. The day after pearl harbor he signed up for duty, joined patton's army and marched across europe. Back home, my grandmother raised their baby and went to work on a bomber assembly line. After the war, they studied on the g.I. Bill, bought a house through fha, and moved west in search of opportunity. And they, too, had big dreams for their daughter, a common dream, born of two continents. My parents shared not only an improbable love; they shared an abiding faith in the possibilities of this nation. They would give me an African name, Barack, or "blessed," believing that in a tolerant America your name is no barrier to success. They imagined me going to the best schools in the land, even though they weren't rich, because in a generous America you don't have to be rich to achieve your potential. They are both passed away now. Yet, I know that, on this night, they look down on me with pride. I stand here today, grateful for the diversity of my heritage, aware that my parents' dreams live on in my precious daughters. I stand here knowing that my story is part of the larger American story, that I owe a debt to all of those who came before me, and that, in no other country on earth, is my story even possible. Tonight, we gather to affirm the greatness of our nation, not because of the height of our skyscrapers, or the power of our military, or the size of our economy. Our pride is based on a very simple premise, summed up in a declaration made over two hundred years ago, "we hold these truths to be self-evident, that all men are created equal. That they are endowed by their creator with certain inalienable rights. That among these are life, liberty and the pursuit of happiness." that is the true genius of America, a faith in the simple dreams of its people, the insistence on small miracles. That we can tuck in our children at night and know they are Fed and clothed and safe from harm. That we can say what we think, write what we think, without hearing a sudden knock on the door. That we can have an idea and start our own business without paying a bribe or hiring somebody's son. That we can participate in the political process without fear of retribution, and that our votes will be counted — or at least, most of the time. This year, in this election, we are called to reaffirm our values and commitments, to hold them against a hard reality and see how we are measuring up, to the legacy of our forbearers, and the promise of future generations. And fellow Americans — Democrats, Republicans, independents — I say to you tonight: we have more work to do. More to do for the workers I met in Galesburg, Illinois, who are losing their union jobs at the maytag plant that's moving to Mexico, and now are having to compete with their own children for jobs that pay seven bucks an hour. More to do for the father I met who was losing his job and choking back tears, wondering how he would pay $4,500 a month for the drugs his son needs without the health benefits he counted on. More to do for the young woman in East St. Louis, and thousands more like her, who has the grades, has the drive, has the will, but doesn't have the money to go to college. Don't get me wrong. The people I meet in small towns and big cities, in diners and office parks, they don't expect government to solve all their problems. They know they have to work hard to get ahead and they want to. Go into the collar counties around Chicago, and people will tell you they don't want their tax money wasted by a welfare agency or the pentagon. Go into any inner city neighborhood, and folks will tell you that government alone can't teach kids to learn. They know that parents have to parent, that children can't achieve unless we raise their expectations and turn off the television sets and eradicate the slander that says a black youth with a book is acting white. No, people don't expect government to solve all their problems. But they sense, deep in their bones, that with just a change in priorities, we can make sure that every child in America has a decent shot at life, and that the doors of opportunity remain open to all. They know we can do better. And they want that choice. In this election, we offer that choice. Our party has chosen a man to lead Us who embodies the best this country has to offer. That man is John Kerry. John Kerry understands the ideals of community, faith, and sacrifice, because they've defined his life. From his heroic service in Vietnam to his years as prosecutor and Lieutenant Governor, through two decades in the United States Senate, he has devoted himself to this country. Again and again, we've seen him make tough choices when easier ones were available. His values and his record affirm what is best in Us. John Kerry believes in an America where hard work is rewarded. So instead of offering tax breaks to companies shipping jobs overseas, he'll offer them to companies creating jobs here at home. John Kerry believes in an America where all Americans can afford the same health coverage our politicians in Washington have for themselves. John Kerry believes in energy independence, so we aren't held hostage to the profits of oil companies or the sabotage of foreign oil fields. John Kerry believes in the constitutional freedoms that have made our country the envy of the world, and he will never sacrifice our basic liberties nor use faith as a wedge to divide Us. And John Kerry believes that in a dangerous world, war must be an option, but it should never be the first option. A while back, I met a young man named shamus at the vfw hall in east moline, Illinois. He was a good-looking kid, 6'2" or 6'3", clear eyed, with an easy smile. He told me he'd joined the marines and was heading to Iraq the following week. As I listened to him explain why he'd enlisted, his absolute faith in our country and its leaders, his devotion to duty and service, I thought this young man was all any of Us might hope for in a child. But then I asked myself: are we serving shamus as well as he was serving Us? I thought of more than 900 service men and women, sons and daughters, husbands and wives, friends and neighbors, who will not be returning to their hometowns. I thought of families I had met who were struggling to get by without a loved one's full income, or whose loved ones had returned with a limb missing or with nerves shattered, but who still lacked long-term health benefits because they were reservists. When we send our young men and women into harm's way, we have a solemn obligation not to fudge the numbers or shade the truth about why they're going, to care for their families while they're gone, to tend to the soldiers upon their return, and to never ever go to war without enough troops to win the war, secure the peace, and earn the respect of the world. Now let me be clear. We have real enemies in the world. These enemies must be found. They must be pursued and they must be defeated. John Kerry knows this. And just as Lieutenant Kerry did not hesitate to risk his life to protect the men who served with him in Vietnam, President Kerry will not hesitate one moment to use our military might to keep America safe and secure. John Kerry believes in America. And he knows it's not enough for just some of Us to prosper. For alongside our famous individualism, there's another ingredient in the American saga. A belief that we are connected as one people. If there's a child on the south side of Chicago who can't read, that matters to me, even if it's not my child. If there's a senior citizen somewhere who can't pay for her prescription and has to choose between medicine and the rent, that makes my life poorer, even if it's not my grandmother. If there's an Arab American family being rounded up without benefit of an attorney or due process, that threatens my civil liberties. It's that fundamental belief — I am my brother's keeper, I am my sisters' keeper — that makes this country work. It's what allows Us to pursue our individual dreams, yet still come together as a single American family. "e pluribus unum." out of many, one. Yet even as we speak, there are those who are preparing to divide Us, the spin masters and negative ad peddlers who embrace the politics of anything goes. Well, I say to them tonight, there's not a liberal America and a conservative America — there's The United States of America. There's not a black America and white America and Latino America and Asian America; there's The United States of America. The pundits like to slice-and-dice our country into red states and blue states; red states for Republicans, blue states for Democrats. But I've got news for them, too. We worship an awesome God in the blue states, and we don't like federal agents poking around our libraries in the red states. We coach little league in the blue states and have gay friends in the red states. There are patriots who opposed the war in Iraq and patriots who supported it. We are one people, all of Us pledging allegiance to the stars and stripes, all of Us defending The United States of America. in the end, that's what this election is about. Do we participate in a politics of cynicism or a politics of hope? John Kerry calls on Us to hope. John Edwards calls on Us to hope. I'm not talking about blind optimism here — the almost willful ignorance that thinks unemployment will go away if we just don't talk about it, or the health care crisis will solve itself if we just ignore it. No, I'm talking about something more substantial. It's the hope of slaves sitting around a fire singing freedom songs; the hope of immigrants setting out for distant shores; the hope of a young naval Lieutenant Bravely patrolling the mekong delta; the hope of a mill worker's son who dares to defy the odds; the hope of a skinny kid with a funny name who believes that America has a place for him, too. The audacity of hope! in the end, that is God's greatest gift to Us, the bedrock of this nation; the belief in things not seen; the belief that there are better days ahead. I believe we can give our middle class relief and provide working families with a road to opportunity. I believe we can provide jobs to the jobless, homes to the homeless, and reclaim young people in cities across America from violence and despair. I believe that as we stand on the crossroads of history, we can make the right choices, and meet the challenges that face Us. America! Tonight, if you feel the same energy I do, the same urgency I do, the same passion I do, the same hopefulness I do — if we do what we must do, then I have no doubt that all across the country, from Florida to Oregon, from Washington to Maine, the people will rise up in November, and John Kerry will be sworn in as president, and John Edwards will be sworn in as Vice President, and this country will reclaim its promise, and out of this long political darkness a brighter day will come. Thank you and God bless you.

speaker: Benjamin McKenzie
text: Thank you. Thank you. Well, it's an unexpected honor to speak to you today. No, really, I mean that. I'm from Texas. I know very little about -- thank you -- I'm from Texas. I know very little about foreign policy, balancing the budgets, or protecting the environment. Of course, you'd be surprised how far you can rise in this country with those qualifications. And, while I may not be an expert on those issues, I definitely do know this -- in 2000, I didn't do anything. I didn't volunteer. I didn't vote. I didn't even register to vote. I was too busy and I thought it didn't matter. And I wasn't the only one. I learned 18 million Americans from 18-24 also didn't vote. Well, it did matter. Fewer Americans have jobs today, and the ones who do are paid less. More Americans, including millions of children, have no healthcare. Our government calls clear-cutting healthy forests and poisoned air clear skies. All over the world, respect for our country has been replaced by fear and confusion. The country can do better. We can do better. This time, this time I am going to vote. When we don't vote, we let someone else take our right to choose. Someone will be elected president, whether we go to the voting booth or not. So, go register. To find out how online go to johnkerry.com. Then on tuesday, November 2, go vote. Even if you have to wait in line for an hour, even if you miss some work, and if you can, do more, tell your friends to vote, tell your family, tell your neighbors. Sign up and volunteer at johnkerry.com. Here's the bottom line -- John Kerry is a war hero, a senator, and a man with a plan to make this country strong and respected. But that won't get him elected. Votes will. The power to change this country, to make it a better place for all of us, isn't up to John Kerry or, frankly, even the Democratic party. It's up to us to put this country back on track. John Kerry needs our help. I'm going to vote for him. And I urge you to join me. Thank you. Thank you.

speaker: Bennie Thompson
text: A bird doesn't sing because it has an answer. It sings because it has a song. It's my honor tonight to introduce the author of these words, an amazing woman of sterling character, sound mind, and a warm heart, who needs no introduction -- dr. Maya Angelou, truly one of the greatest poets of the last century, dr. Angelou's words have given voice to the mute, zeile to the complacent, and comfort to the forlorn. Ladies and gentlemen, please e join me in welcoming the former poet laureate of the United States, dr. Maya Angelou.

speaker: Betsy Cavendish
text: Today, I speak, and ask this convention to speak, for millions of women who want their freedom protected. In this election, Americans will decide the future of a woman's right to choose. This election will empower the victor to name new justices to The Supreme Court. This election will determine the choices available to the next generation of American women. If John Kerry is President for the next four years, this fundamental freedom will be safe. If he's not, it will be taken away. It really is that simple. It really is that stark. And it really is that important. The choice is clear -- John Kerry trusts women, not the government, to determine the course of our lives. John Kerry knows that privacy and self-determination are cherished American values, and he will protect, not diminish, them. John Kerry will respect our basic privacy rights, not subpoena women's private medical records. in the Kerry-Edwards administration, abortion will become much more rare because instead of limiting access to birth control, John Kerry will expand access to contraception at home and around the world. Instead of holding science hostage to anti-choice ideology, he will free researchers to discover new cures for disease. John Kerry would never pack the federal courts with anti-choice judges, never stack federal agencies with fervent opponents of choice, and never limit our freedoms. Because my mother's generation stood up and told the government to stay out of our private decisions, I grew up free to choose. Now, I'm standing up and supporting John Kerry and John Edwards to make sure my daughters, Margaret and Lucy, don't have to fight that battle all over again. And we are not standing alone. More than one million pro-choice Americans in the largest demonstration ever marched on Washington this spring to stand up for choice. In 99 days, we will stand together again and send John Kerry to The White House. It's the right choice for protecting our freedoms, the right way to protect our future.

speaker: Carol Moseley Braun
text: God bless you. God bless you. Thank you. Thank you. I am honored, I am honored to have this chance to say thank you to all of you, the delegates to the 44th Democratic convention. Thank you for your activism. Thank you for making this convention a turning point for America. Thank you for working to Elect John Kerry as the next President of the United States. I come to you as the granddaughter of edna and thomas davie. Thomas davie was a patriot who served his country in world war I and who was prepared to make the ultimate sacrifice for America. He did not have the right to vote. In fact, he had no rights. But his patriotism survived the hard rock of segregation because he believed that his sacrifice would help make things better for the next generation. And that the future he sought to create would be better than the past he could not change. He fought to give us freedom from fear and want. He fought to secure for us the freedom of faith and expression and speech that are the light of our world. He fought for an America that did not yet exist but that he believed was possible. And he was right. I stand here today as a beneficiary of his sacrifice and of the sacrifices of generations of patriots who dreamed of making the way better for me and for you than it was for them. I've served my country as an assistant U.S.. Attorney, as state legislator, as a county executive, as A United States senator, and as A United States ambassador. I have had opportunities my grandfather could not have imagined, but in his time, he worked to move this country in the direction of its highest ideals. The future he fought for is the present we inherited. It is now our time and our turn to create a future we'll be proud to leave to the next generation of Americans. We have a choice. We have a choice. We can either give them an America of opportunity, or we can allow others to turn the lights out on the American dream. We can protect their personal freedoms or resign ourselves to fear and insecurity . Nothing less, delegates, nothing less than our duty to our ancestors and to our children is at stake in this November election, and nothing is more stark than the contrast between the potential of John Kerry and the track record of this current administration. I worked with John Kerry in the United States Senate for six years, and I had a chance to get to know him as only a colleague can. Based on that experience, I can stand here today and say to you, without reservation, that in John Kerry we have a chance to put a patriot in the White House who will save our great country and put us on a path of peace, prosperity, and progress. We have the opportunity to give our great country a great president. Now I did not know John Kerry as a decorated hero who served two tours of duty in Vietnam, but when I put myself forward as a candidate for your nomination, I met some of the men who had served with him. Including a member of my own Illinois delegation, by the way. Those Vietnam veterans, black and white and brown, hold a special place in my heart, and I'm sure in yours, too. Most importantly, they know -- they know the real deal. They know the genuine article. They know the truth. And they hold John Kerry in a special place in their hearts, because of who he is, because of his courage and his spirit and what he means to our country. John Kerry will make America safer. And he will give us a strong and sensible foreign policy. He will restore America's honor and regard in the world by respecting the aspirations of the world's people and by working with others to address the global issues of health and human rights, the environment and education and trade. He will make job creation here at home a priority, and I know he will work for pay equity and fair representation for women and for the equal rights amendment. He will restore our confidence that those freedoms my grandfather cherished are still alive and well in America. John Kerry has the capacity and the commitment to create an America we will be proud to pass on to the next generation. But he has to get elected. He has to get elected. Delegates, you will leave this convention with a nominee and a message that can inspire the American people and revitalize their faith that this country can work for them. You can assure them that Democrats will reward the hard work of millions of everyday people by restoring the greatest values of the greatest country in the world. You -- you can give our country better leadership than we have today. And that is why I want to thank you. Because you are the patriots who will make John Kerry the next President of the United States. You are the patriots who will keep faith with the sacrifice that my grandfather and all our ancestors made last century, and that is being made even today by young men and women who are in harm's way in what is still a dangerous world. You are the patriots who will inspire every voter to go to the polls and then make sure every vote gets counted. You are the hope of our nation. Thank you for your work. Thank you for your dedication and your service. I feel good about this election. I see a bright day ahead. I see an America on sure-footed, heading in a direction we will be happy to meet. Because of you, I see John Kerry in the White House as the next President of the United States of America. Thank you and God bless you. Thank you.

speaker: Chris Heinz
text: Good evening. It's an honor to be here tonight to introduce my mother, Teresa Heinz Kerry. But first I'd like to say how proud I am of my stepfather and how honored I am to serve this cause. When my mom first introduced me to John, I said to myself, self, the only man good enough for your mother is the President of the United States. I think it's going to work out. More seriously, last week our family lost a dear friend, Joe Clark. Joe worked with our family for 35 years, since July of 1966. He was family. Joe first met my mom when she was newly married and pregnant with my older brother. Digesting the loss of our friend, my mom and I spoke last week at the beginning of her American journey. We talked about the 38 years that have passed in her life. And as Joe would say, all them changes. You see, in 1966, my mom was new to America, having recently arrived from her Native home of mozambique. Bright, 27 years young, and speaking five, five languages as a trained translator. She'd Mitt him on the -- yeah. She'd met a man from Pittsburgh, fallen in love, and moved to The United States to start a new life. And as so frequently happens in this country, and an immigrant, she took America and its opportunities and freedoms for all their worth. Both as a mother and as an engaged progressive person. And a long career dedicated to improving the lives of others, her finest work has been the stewardship of the Heinz endowment since my father's death in 1991. She is a true visionary. "The New York Times" has refered to her as one of the nation's leading philanthropists. But don't take their word for it or mine. I'm definitely biased. Simply go ask the residents of western Pennsylvania and beyond about her -- about her tireless efforts to protect our environment, promote the arts, improve education, and broaden economic opportunity.
