She used to scrub floors as a domestic worker, put a cleaning rag in a pocketbook and ride the subways in Brooklyn so I could have food on the table. She taught me as I walked into that subway that life is about not where you start, but where you're going. That's family values. And I wanted somebody in my community -- I wanted to show that example as I ran for president, I hoped that one child that come out of the ghetto like I did could look at me, walk across the stage, with governors and senators and know they didn't have to be a drug dealer. They didn't have to be a hood lum. They didn't have to be a gangsta. They could stand up from a broken home on welfare and they could run for President of the United States. As you know, I Li in New York. I -- I flive New York. I was there September 11 when that despicable act of terrorism happened. A few days after I left home, my family had taken in a young man who lost his family. I had to do a radio show that morning. When I got there my friend said reverend, we are going to start at a certain time and sing a song. We are dedicating it to the victims of 9/11. I said, what song are you playing? He said, we are playing, "America the beautiful. " they played that rendition sung by Ray Charles. As you know, we lost ray a few weeks ago, but I sat there that morning and listened to ray speak, o beautiful for spacious skies, for ample waves of grain, purple mountain majesty across the fruited eplain. He wasn't singing about what he knew. Ray had been blind since he was a child. He hadn't seen many fruited plains. He was singing about what he believed to be. Mr. President, we love America not because of all of us have seen the beauty all the time. But we believed if we kept on working, if we kept on marching, if we kept on voting, if we kept on believing, we would make America beautiful for everybody. Start November let's make America beautiful again. Thank you and God bless you.

speaker: Amy Klobuchar
text: I bring you greetings from the state of Minnesota. Where in the words of Garieson Keeler, Lake Wobe gone's poet lawyer yet, all the women are strong, all the men are good looking and all the voters are above average. Minnesota is also home to a proud Democratic tradition. The integrity of walter mondale who is here with us today the optimism of Herbert Humphrey and Paul wellstone. This year we found these qualities in John Kerry and John Edwards. I come before you today as a prosecutor. in the past five years in hennepin county, we have joined with the police and the community and business to reduce serious crime by more than 25%. Yo know, people think the prosecutors see every conviction and every arrest for a cause of celebration. But I'll tell you this, every time someone gets arrested, every time they get convicted, it is not a cause for celebration. It is a failure. It is failure for the individual. It's a failure for the family. And it's a failure for the nation. Democrats know that the best way to fight crime is not only to be tough on criminals, but also to invest in kids. We want to see our children in the classroom, not in the courtroom. We want to stheem on the playground, not on the prison grounds. As a young prosecutor, John Kerry saw firsthand the impact of crime on kids and neighborhoods. He saw it and he did something about it. And John Kerry not only went after street crime, he went after organized crime and corporate crime. When a c.e.o. Steals from his company, we should go after that c.e.o. Just like we go after someone who steals a car off the street. When a company -- when a company robs its workers' pension fund, we should go after it just like we give chase to a guy who robs a bank with a mask. As John Edwards reminds us every day, we cannot have two systems of justice, one for the rich and powerful, and one for everyone else. We are one America. We have one system of justice. There is a famous texan who once said something very profound. No. It wasn't George Bush. It was the late Congresswoman Barbara Jordan who said what the American people want is simple. We want a nation that's as good as its promise and today we need a President And a Vice President Who will make good on that promise, the promise of a stronger and more secure America. They are America's future and their names are John Kerry and John Edwards. Thank you very much.

speaker: Angelo Kyle
text: Thank you. My name is Angelo kyle. I'm President of the National Association of Counties. Which represents America's 3,066 county governments. I am also proud to be an elected county commissioner from Lake County, Illinois! The county nestled between Chicago and the Wisconsin border. Let me extend a special greeting to the more than 200 convention delegates who are also elected county officials. As they and so many of you might know, county governments play a vital and growing role in the lives of America's families, bringing crucial services to communities from rural America to our suburbs and central cities. When Americans need a police officer, a firefighter, or an emergency medical technician, they call upon county government. When Americans commute to work or drive their sons and daughters to school or a soccer game, they take county highways and county bridges. When Americans face health emergencies, more often than not, they depend on our county hospitals. When Americans seek fair hearings in our great judicial system, they go to our county courts. When Americans go to the polls in November, those polls will be overseen by a county government. On September 11, county governments and county workers were the first responders to serve our families well. America's counties need effective partners in the federal government and The White House, more and more our local and national governments depend on each other, from homeland security to the running of elections, Americans demand and expect county governments and federal governments to work together. I believe John Kerry and John Edwards will provide that cooperation, commitment, and leadership. If America is to remain strong and secure, if America is to remain fair and just, if America is to remain examinationity and caring -- compassionate and caring, then we must have strong leadership from county governments throughout America. And we must have strong leadership and proven national leaders in the White House. We must have strong leadership from John Kerry and John Edwards. God bless all of you. God bless America's counties. And God bless America! Thank you very much!

speaker: Ashley Bell
text: Friends, Democrats, fellow Americans, now is the time that we must rededicate ourselves to the young sons and daughters of our nation. My name is ashleigh bell and I'm a son -- my name is Ashley Bell and I'm loyal to this crowd and a candidate for general assembly in the statehouse. As a teen, neighborhood Democrats taught me that no matter how big or small your town, or what side of town you live on, we're all just neighbors. Growing up, I saw that Democrats were the people you trusted to do what's right for everybody. And today we believe that streets, highways, and railroad tracks are not what divide us. They are what connect us all. Today, no American, young or old, can deny we flive divided times. We -- we live in divided times. We were promises bridges to connect us would be built, but instead barriers to separate us have taken their place. But I know and trust that John Kerry will unite us again as a leader of 50 states, not 50%. A President Of red and blue states -- of not red and blue states, but for red, white, and blue. For all our states, for our people, for our one America that we all believe in. I serve as the national President of the college Democrats of America. Together we represent the efforts and the ideals of millions of college students on campuses across this great country. And today I have the pleasure of introducing a film. It's called "if I had a minute with the president." this is fitting because the values of our party are like those of our nation. They're ageless. I present to you America's children, unscripted, with thoughts of what they would share with the President If they had a minute. Thank you.

speaker: Bill Richardson
text: As Chairman Of this con vention, as a proud -- as Chairman Of this convention, as a proud New Mexico Democrat, I thank you for the great honor you have given me as the first Hispanic chair of a party convention. Yes. >> from my experience, as a congressman, as a U.n. Ambassador, and a cabinet secretary, I have come to one unshakable conclusion about our nation's foreign policy and national security. Whether you are a Democrat or a Republican, at the end of the day, our nation is strongest and better served when our President Is respected and admired in the world. Franklin roosevelt's courage and perseverance turned the tide against facism. John Kennedy's energy and vision lifted New York new heights as a beacon for all that was good and possible in the world. And Bill Clinton, his hope and certainty led our nation and the world to an era of unprecedented peace and prosperity. Their leadership kept America strong. And respected in the world. in the eyes of friend and foe alike. Their leadership made our nation a force for moving the human condition forward. For peace. For optimism. For opportunity. It's time for that kind of leadership again. It's time for a President Who believes deeply that from those who much is given, much is expected. It is time for John Kerry. As a young man, John Kerry enlisted in the Navy and volunteered for the front lines. And ever since then he has served his nation, and thing up for America's security, defending our values, and never faltering in his resolve to keep America safe, secure, and strong. It's time for John Kerry. My fellow Americans, I don't need to tell you that the world is a dangerous place. We are reminded of that every day of our lives. We've seen it on the streets and in the subways and airports outside this convention. Al qaeda is the heir to enemies who have defeated us before. Today's equivalent of nazrs and fascists who hate the values at the core of the American way of life. Democracy and plurelism, diversity and tolerance, invasion and achievement, strength and -- innovation and achievement, strength and freedom and the fate of cade clade be the same as those other tyrants and thugs. We will defeat them. And we need a President To lead that fight. It's time for John Kerry. Right now America's strength is measured in tanks, aircraft, ships, and missiles has never been greater. But our strength is measured by our standing in the world is at an all-time low. Today because of the administration's go-it-alone foreign policy, we are, not surprisingly, on our known a dangerous world. We pursue the enemies of civilization with few partners. We are not a safe and secure as we deserve to be. We don't have the leadership that America needs. It's time for John Kerry. Just four years ago I was able to go anywhere in the world, meet with allies, former adversaries, and even our sworn enemies and get them to do things that they often did not want to do, but that clearly advanced the security of the United States and the stability of the world. I was able to negotiate the lease of hostages -- release of hostages and American service flen dictators and thugs. I was able to unite The United Nations Security Council around American interests and solidify sometimes shaky ties with our allies in latin America, asia and africa. I was able to do all that because American strength was understood. American integrity was clear. American leadership was just. And American leadership was respected. After four years, a lot of that goodwill has disappeared. This administration has failed to understand what commanders in chief of both parties have understood for years. Power breeds indifference. And power without respect breeds con tempt. -- contempt. Because this White House decided to go it alone, we are shouldering the burden of Iraq alone. We are paying the bills for the war nearly $200 billion so far, alone. Our troops are being stretched thin. Our deficit is ballooning. And we are distracted from the important work of chasing Al Qaeda and destroying it once and for all. My friends, we need a President Who understands that America must be strong and respected in the world. We need a President Who will unite the world in the war against terrorism. It is time for John Kerry. He will keep our military strong, but unlike our current president, John Kerry will also lead trong alliances to fight terrorism and keep America safe. Strong, respected. That was the America I represented around the world. That is the America that John Kerry will build. My fellow American peoples, it is time for a sensible and reliable America that respects its friends and alliances. For a fair and upright America that goes to war only when we need to not because we want to. We are a noble and thoughtful America where the President Respects responsibility and demands accountability when things don't go according to plan. For in America where the President Understands the price of war and the price of not planning for peace. My friends, it's time for new leadership in America. It's time for a new president. Now more than ever it is the time for John Kerry! Thank you very much. God bless America and God bless New Mexico.

speaker: Bob Graham
text: Thank you. Fellow Democrats, fellow Democrats, thank you. And I especially want to thank the wonderful floridians who are here tonight. I want to thank you for granting me the honor and the privilege of serving you for almost four decades. My family and I are so grateful to all of you for having given us the wonderful opportunity to share an adventure in public service because of you. Florida and America, Florida has made a difference to me. I know that we are going to make a difference for John Kerry and John Edwards. And, friends, this time when the votes are counted, we are going to make a huge difference in Florida for America. My fellow Americans, I want to tell you why I'm going to cast my ballot for John Kerry and John Edwards. The preamble to the constitution of the United States tells us that one of the most important responsibilities of government is to provide for the common defense. Friends, I want to ask you to think about this. Think about this. It has been over 1,000 days -- 1,000 days since terrorists attacked us on September 11 and changed our nation. 1,000 days after pearl harbor America had landed its troops on the beaches of normandy and we were rolling to victory in world war II. in the same amount of time in this new war on terror we have not yet secured the beachhead. John Kerry and John Edwards will. Friends, in this new century we have seen the rise of perilous new threats and yet we have not stopped them. We haven't even stood up to them. John Kerry and John Edwards will. At a time when freedom-loving people around the world are looking for leader snip the war against terror, -- leadership in the war against terror, America has not produced it. My friends, John Kerry and John Edwards will. As Governor Of Florida, I learned that the f.b.I. And the c.I.a. Failed to communicate with state and local law enforcement agencies, the very agencies who are the frontline of our defense against terrorist attacks. As Florida's senator, I saw seaports where the greatest security was often little more than a chain-link fence. As a member of The Senate Intelligence Committee, I have seen the places in the world where the worst biological weapons were manufactured. And where nuclear materials go largely unprotected and where the next generation of terrorists is being recruited. And as Chairman Of The Senate Intelligence Committee I investigated the September 11 tragedy. And I saw how that tragedy should have been prevented. From all of my service, I come to this conclusion, yes, there are real threats, but also, friends, there are real solutions. Just last week the 9/11 commission joined a series of previous commissions in making its recommendations for major change in the way we fight the war on terror. Few of these recommendations are new. Most are obvious. But sadly, 1,000 days after September 11 nub of those recommendations is -- none of those recommendations is in place. The ideas are there. It is the leadership which has been missing. Friends, we know that North Korea and Iran have nuclear aspirations. If not nuclear weapons. And yet only John Kerry and John Edwards have a plan. A plan to keep the deadliest weapons from falling into the hands of the world's most dangerous people. We know that money is the terrorist's fountain and lifeline. And yet it was John Kerry long before September 11 who had a plan to cut off the sources of terrorist funding. We know, friends, that our bridges, our tunnels, our trains, our buses, our chemical plants, our food and water supplies are still vullnorblee attack. And -- vulnerable to attack. And yet only John Kerry and John Edwards willig to make the investment -- willing to make the investment to make us truly safe. And, friends, we know, Iraq did not attack The United States on September 11. It was oo sama Bin forgotten and Al Qaeda that attacked us. That is why John Kerry and John Edwards will not only win the peace in Iraq, but they will carry the fight and the war on terror wherever it needs to be fought. Whether it is the palaces of the middle east or the banks of europe or the sea ports of Florida or the firehouses of Boston. John Kerry recognizes that victory in the war on terror will require all the resources of the United States of America. Diplomatically. Economically. Intelligence, and military. Today, my friends, recruiting billboards for Al Qaeda are being erected on the main streets of the major cities of the middle east. We need to work with our allies. We need to work with like-minded people in the Islamic world to tear down those recruiting billboards and to clean the swamp of terror. Providing for the common defense is not just a piece of rhetoric from one of our founding documents. It is the most solemn responsibility we entrust to our national leaders. This is a war that demands new resources and new ideas. But most of all, most of all it is a war that demands new leadership. And when Americans ask the question, who will provide that leadership, I can tell you, John Kerry and John Edwards broil vide that leadership. They will for our children, for our grandchildren, for our security, for America and for the world. We must Elect John Kerry as the next President of the United States of America and we will. Thank you. Thank you.

speaker: Carolyn McCarthy
text: 11 years ago, gun violence changed my life dramatically. The nation called it the long island railroad massacre. It claimed the lives of six people and injured 19 others. I lost my husband, Dennis, on that day. My son Kevin was severely wounded. But I took the sorrow of that tragedy and turned it into strength that helped the press the first assault weapons ban. Senator John Kerry stood with us then to pass that ban. On the manufacture and sale of 19 military-style assault weapons. And today I stand with John Kerry, the next President of the United States. Incredibly on September 13, 2004, 48 days from today, this ban expires. And these weapons will again be flooding our streets. We don't have to go to Iraq to find weapons of mass destruction. Once again, criminals will be able to buy them openly in our own communities. Our children will once again face the threat of these weapons. And so will our police officers. Assault weapons kill one in every five officers who die in the line of duty. Four years ago George Bush promised to sign legislation to extend the assault weapons ban, but today he has yet to ask his Republican leadership in Congress to take action and extend the ban. I know President John Kerry will fight for a ban on assault weapons. John Kerry and I share the value of our police officers and we share the values of millions of Americans across our nation who know that the assault weapons ban is working. If you care about gun violence, about our children, about our police officers on the front lines every day, and if you believe, as Senator Kerry and I do, that military-style assault weapons do not belong on our streets or in our neighborhoods, then I ask you to call The White House. Call the speaker of The House of Representatives and demand -- demand that Congress serve the American people and vote to extend this ban before September 13. Nothing is more important than protecting innocent families from needless, preventable gun violence. And thank you John Kerry, our next president, for standing with the American families against this violence. Thank you so very much. God bless America. Hey, New York.

speaker: Cate Edwards
text: Hi. I'm Cate Edwards. By now I'm not sure if more of you know me as John and Elizabeth's daughter or Jack and Emma Clair's old hest sister but tonight I can tell you I'm the proud child of two people who made our house one of hope and who will make sure our land is promise and possibility when my dad is the next Vice President of the United States of America. And standing beside him tonight, standing beside him always is his wife, my mom, Elizabeth. Both my mom and my dad have always taught me to dream big and reach high. Now as a young woman about to head out into the real world, I have one thought on my mind, if I can live a life even half as accomplished as my mother's, then I will have done all right. The truth is my mom has really done it all. She's been a star law student and a p.t.a. Member, a successful attorney and a coach, a public servant and a mentor. And above all, a mother who is always there for us, always. But I look up to her not only because of what she does with her life, but because of the way she lives it, with the strength that inspires, with the courage that carries on, with the kindness that keeps our front door open and our family and friends close. And with an optimism that keeps her joined to my father at the heart. I know some may find it easier to look at the hurt in the world and the challenges around us and simply turn away or sit at home. But we were raised by our parents to believe differently because my mom and dad believe differently. John Kerry and Teresa believe differently, too, and that's why together we're going to change this country. They say a ship in a harbor is safe. That's not what ships are built for. They're built for exploring new possibilities and to quote from my mom's favorite poem, they're built for allowing us to believe that a further shore is reachable from here. My mom believes. She's brought joy to our home and love to our hearts. And she will join my father in bringing hope to America when she is the next second lady of the United States. Ladies and gentlemen, my mother, Elizabeth Edwards.

speaker: Chaka Fattah
text: Can hear that great American Robert Kennedy when he said our future may be beyond our vision, but it is not completely beyond our control. It is, he said, the shaping impulse of America that neither fate nor nature nor the irresistible tides of history, but the work of our own hands matched to reason and principle that will determine our destiny. Democrats have always worked to make the potential that education holds for our children real here in America. Tonight over 1. 5 million young people are working to prepare themselves for college through the national gear up program that I worked to establish with a bipartisan group of my colleagues and to expand with the active assistance of Senator Kerk and Senator John Edwards. -- Senator Kerry and Senator John Edwards. These people are not future Democrats or future Republicans, they are the future architects of our nation. On education, Democrats have a different approach. We believe that we shouldn't have resources without reform because that would be a waste of money. But that we can have reform without resources because that would waste a much more important and much more precious resource, our childrens' future. It is simple. If children are going to be tested, they should have qualified teachers. If children are expected to learn, they have a right to expect a textbook. If they are expected to compete in a high-tech world, then they must have computers and full access to the internet. You see, Democrats have a common sense approach to education reform. For once, we are saying that we should provide in our lowest-achieving schools the same level resource that we provide in our highest-achieving schools. The election of John Kerry and John Edwards, I would tell you is inextricably intertwined to the future in which each child and every child will have an equal opportunity to learn. The presidency of skwlerk allow tens of millions of -- of John Kerry will allow tens of millions of young people, as dr. King said, to be the ark tects of their own lives. We know as Democrats that education is less costly than ignorance. It is not just the next election, it is, indeed, the next generation. Thank you. Thank you, Pennsylvania. Thank you, Philadelphia. And let's March on to victory.

speaker: Charles Rangel
text: God Bless America. God bless this great convention. Right here in this state, in this great city, in this village, a revolution was started. People came to this country seeking a better way of life, religious freedom, economic opportunity, but somehow england mistreated these people, disrespected these people, taxed them but didn't give them the benefits that they deserved, and attempted to take away their self-esteem and respect. These people, these patriots got mad as hell and they said that they weren't taking it anymore. And it's here that the revolution started. As a result of their courage, they all didn't have guns, but they picked up what they had, and they made it abundantly clear that from this time their vision was going to create an America that, for all of us, we will enjoy the benefits of this great legacy. And all of us that are here today, we stand on somebody's shoulders. We're here today because someone built the railroads and the bridges and picked the Cotton. We're today because people fought for our civil rights and our voting rights. We're here because people cared for us and this great country gave us hope and vision for the future. But my fellow Americans, the things that we fought for are in jeopardy. And we have an obligation to leave an America stronger and not threatened than the one that we've got. And who d we look for? We cannot look for the patriots of 1775. We are the patriots for the next revolution which takes place in Boston and takes place with Senator Kerry and takes place with Senator Edwards. We're mad as hell and this is the revolution that we would win for us, for our party, and for our children. Look and see who we are. We are America. We're black and we're white. We're brown and we're yellow. We are jews and Squean tiles. Cath -- and we're gentiles. Catholics and protestants. My brothers and my sisters, we are America. And when anyone threatens us, they threaten our children and our grandchildren. Senator Kerry and Senator Edwards that we patriots are giving the mandate, not with bullets, but with ballots to bring this country around to what she has always been. I share with you one thing. And that is the viss of the future that -- the vision of the future that these new leaders will bring. We should let the whole country know that we are mad as hell that our kids have to join the military because they can't find a jobe. We are mad as hell that our senior citizens aren't able to get the health care and prescription drugs they need. We are mad as hell that the respect we used to enjoy throughout the world is being tampered. And we're mad as hell question go into war with no information, bad information, and still not have a way out of it. Senator Kennedy, Senator Kerry, and our president, when he becomes president, and our Vice President Will restore the dignity that our country once enjoyed. We will be respected around the world as we pull out and restore some type of order and bring peace and trang quill -- tranquility to the world. Our young people will know what it is like to get an education, a job, and dignity. And we stand on their shoulders, but our shoulders will be big enough so that others will be able to appreciate what President Kerry and Vice President Edwards will do. This is the time for us to be mad as hell. This is the time for us to pick up the ballots and register gone and to every town, every village, every city and every state to say, this land is our land. This country is our country. That flag belongs to us and not to them. And we will fight and we will win and we will continue. God Bless America. God bless Senator Kerry. God bless Senator Edwards. Let's continue to struggle. The revolution begins and, once again, we will win. God bless you.

speaker: Charles Schumer
text: Well, thank you and thank you New York. Thank you America. Now, ladies and gentlemen, honored guests, delegates, before I begin I want to mention someone who we new yorkers and all Americans miss dearly at this convention. And that is Daniel Patrick Moynihan. Senator Moynihan was one of the greatest intellectual leaders in government that our country has ever seen and he would have wanted nothing more than to see John Kerry elected president. Pat, we miss you. Now, ladies and gentlemen, this November America will hold its first presidential election since those horrendous terrorist attacks of September 11. That so deeply affected our country, especially my home state of New York. So many of us in New York think of those attacks every day. We think of them because we know that we must do everything we can to prevent them from ever happening again. Here in this hall we have no doubt who will make America safer, stronger, and more secure. John Kerry and John Edwards. We know that John Kerry a decorated war veteran who boldly served and sacrificed for his country. We know that John Kerry is a tough leader. Who can make those tough decisions. A passionate patriot who won't shrink from doing what is necessary to keep us safe. John Kerry understands that we need a strong foreign policy to prevent terrorists from taking advantage of the openness that we cherish. We must prevent them from continuing to attack us. But he also knows we need a multilateral foreign policy because no country, no matter how strong, can fight this war on terror alone. John Kerry is for strength, but he's for bringing all the nations of the world together. John Kerry also understands that to win the war on terror, we need a President Who will focus on the war overseas and dedicate far more resources and far more effort to defend our homeland. Our ports, our railways, our bridges. But when we propose protecting commercial airplanes from shoulder-launched missiles, this administration said no. John Kerry will say yes. When we proprose viding detection devices to keep nuclear materials out of our ports, they said no. John Kerry says yes. When we propose efforts to protect our subways and trains and mass transit, they said no. John Kerry will say yes. And one final thing. Veterans. Veterans can rest assured that John Kerry, unlike this administration, John Kerry will never forget the service they have rendered to our country. He says to veterans, he will never shortchange you on health care or cut corners on your Ben Fitz. -- benefits. He will honor our nation's debt for your service veterans. So, ladies and gentlemen, when it comes to a stronger, safer, and more secure America, the choice is clear. John Kerry will keep America safe. John Kerry will keep America strong. And John Kerry is the right choice for America's safety, security, and strength. God bless you and on to victory in November.

speaker: Cheryl Jacques
text: Hello. I'm Cheryl Jacques. On behalf of my partner, Jennifer, and our beautiful twin boys, timmy and tommy, I'd like to say how proud I am to be back in my home state of Massachusetts where I had the privilege of serving in the state Senate for more than a decade. America has often been called a great experiment, what began with a revolution against tyranny right here in Massachusetts has transformed into an evolution of progress, progress is not a ceiling to reach, it is an infinite frontier, gay, Lesbian, Bisexual, and transgender Americans share the dream, the dream of a better, stronger, and more united America. We protect our country. We die for our country. That's why we seek the right to serve openly and honestly in our armed forces, to defend our freedoms and the rights of all American families. We see a health care crisis that can be alleviated through more personal responsibility. That in part is why we're working for marriage equality so we can do -- so we can do what families do best, care for each other in sickness and in health. We want a fully fortified battle against HIV-AIDS that spears this generation the ravages of the last. We want to ensure that violent hate crimes are aggressively prosecuted. We are optimistic. We are innovators. We envision new technologies, cures for diseases, and better products to enrich people's lives. We work hard. We contribute to our communities. We pay our fair share of taxes. And we simply ask that we're never fired because of who we are. Today in 36 states in this country, 36 states, it is legal to fire the star employee simply because the boss thinks he or she is gay. That is wrong. That is wrong. There are some who criticize our American dream, and that is their right. And this flag on my lapel shields me from neither criticism nor dissent. This flag is a clarion call to every human being that equality and freedom also triumph over oppression and discrimination. John Kerry and John Edwards share the dream. John Kerry and John Edwards share the dream of a better America, with schools that inspire, an economy that roars, homeland security that comes first, and equal rights for all Americans. They know. They know that the constitution is a vessel of freedom, not a tool for discrimination. Together we will send a message for all Americans to hear that the light of inclusion will once again wipe away the darkness of division. Thank you and God bless America.

speaker: Ciro Rodriguez
text: Fellow delegates, good afternoon. We come from all walks of life with the words of latinos, African-Americans or anglos, we're all Democrats, committed to putting our country back on the right path. When we look through the prism of American society, we see our greatest reflection in our diversity. We are Americans bound together with shared beliefs. We're joined by our common greatness for a bright future, but we must harness the potential of our diversity if we're to become strong again. With President Kerry, we can and will make America strong. Kerry and Edwards know that a strong America must have a military that is best in the world. Kerry and Edwards know that a strong America helps all students graduate from high school and make a higher education truly affordable. Kerry and Edwards know that a strong America provides our children with access to quality health care and low health care prices. Kerry and Edwards knows that a strong America has an economy that supports jobs here at home so we have the tools to advance and meet the challenges ahead. Kerry and Edwards also know that a strong America respects its veterans and war heroes. We need new leadership in the White House to make America strong again. In south Texas, I've seen the hopes and fears in the faces of young parents, worry about our children's future. From the border counties of Texas to San Antonio, I've heard the voices crying for change in seeking opportunities to succeed. We must build a stronger America. We must invest in our future. We must invest in ourselves, save schools -- safe schools, good air and water, good roads, energy independence, together as Democrats let's make America strong again. We have the ticket for success, John Kerry and John Edwards will lead America so that we can stand strong again at home and abroad. .

speaker: Claudia Kennedy
text: In 1995, with chaos and violence sweeping the balkans, The United States decided that our security and our values demanded that we intervene. But rather than go it alone, President Clinton and general John Shalikashvili, then Chairman Of the joint chiefs, enlisted the support of NATO in the effort to achieve peace. Together the atlantic alliance lifted the siege of sarajevo and formed the dayton peace accords. America rallied the forces of freedom and in return wered the respect of the world. General shalikashvili earned his commission as an officer in the United States army in 1959. Through four decades he served all across the globe, from Vietnam to europe to Iraq. Under the first President Bush, he became supreme allied commander europe and commander in chief of the United States European command. In 1993, he became the 13th Chairman Of the joint chiefs of staff, the highest ranking officer in the United States military. A visionary leader, he has led the world's strongest military with honor and distinction. And with a great love for the nation he served. Ladies and gentlemen, it is my great honor to introduce to you the former Chairman Of the joint chiefs of staff, general John Shalikashvili. >> thank you, general Kennedy. Thank you very much. It's a great honor to stand here before you this evening. And to experience firsthand this enormous energy of democracy in action. But I do not stand here as a political figure, rather I'm here as an old soldier and a new Democrat. I am a new Democrat because I believe strongly that John Kerry and John Edwards are the right choice for American security and the right choice for America. We live in a dangerous time. Terrorists have attacked our -- here at home. And they continue to strike around the world. And the greatest danger before us is that these terrorists will somehow get their hands on weapons of mass destruction. And if that were not enough, some nations continue to threaten regional stability while pursuing their own nuclear ambitions which threaten all of us. But throughout his campaign, John Kerry has shown time and again that he understands these dangers and that he's fully prepared for the challenges ahead. He knows that to be truly safe at home we must significantly strengthen protection here at home. And that we must not again allow ourselves to be distracted from the relentless pursuit of these terrorists. At this very moment as we meet here, thousands upon thousands of our brave troops are deployed in Afghanistan and in Iraq in a protracted and bloody struggle, still others remain deployed around the world, upholding the cause of freedom and representing what is the very best about America. And you know, John Kerry was the first to warn that these worldwide military deployments are dangerously overstretching our military and particularly our army. And that unless we increase the size of the army and restructure it to give it new capabilities needed in this new war against terrorism, we are in real danger of returning to the days of the hollow army. And John Kerry has also made it crystal clear that no matter how strong we might be, success in a war on terror or in bringing peace and stability to Afghanistan and to Iraq will likely allude us unless we bring France and allies to our side, both for the fight and for the long hard work of reconstruction. We must do this not because we need somebody's approval when we act to protect our security, but because we are more effective when friends and allies stand by our side. As together we share the burden and the risks and the opportunities. There is absolutely no doubt that capable allies and strong alliances are today more important to our security than ever before. I am no stranger to war. Before I was 10 years old, I had lived through the brutal occupation of Poland, the country of my birth, and the totally destruction of my hometown during the 1944 Warsaw uprising. Years later, like John Kerry and so many other young Americans, I participated in a very different kind of a war in the Rice paddies and jungles of Vietnam. And still later at the end of desert storm when Saddam Hussein, with unbelievable brutality, turned against the Kurds, I was asked to lead an international operation to stop the dying and the misery and to return home some 700,000 Kurds to what was left of the villages and their homes. I know -- I know about the horror of war. And thus join with others like John Kerry in believing that we must go to war only, only when all other efforts to restore -- to resolve the threats to us have been exhausted. And only then, only then would going to war becomes absolutely necessary, then to go with full resolve and to use force decisively. But never, never should we go to war without a comprehensive plan for how to secure the peace once military victory has been won. While I know the dark side of war, I also know firsthand about the bright side of America, the America that from its earliest days has been a land of boundless opportunity and a beacon of hope and liberty around the world. This is the America that we cherish, you and I, and that we defend. And that is the America that John Kerry will lead. You know, from my first days as a private in basic training, I've always been enormously proud to be an American soldier. In my eyes, the truth were known, in my eyes there is no higher title. And for 39 years I had the great privilege to serve in the company of such heroes, ordinary men and women whose selfless courage and love of country befit this extraordinary nation of ours. In places like Kabul and Kandahar and fallujah and tikrit and the thousand others we can hardly pronouns, they're writing their own page in the glorious history of American fighting men and women. They fought and they bled and too many of them died. And all they have ever asked in return was that we lead them well, that we train them for the tasks at hand, equip them properly and give them enough men and mateiiel to get the job done and to protect themselves and their bodies. And the only other thing they ask is that when we send them into harm's way, we take care of their families here back home. That is all. That is all. And you and I are so incredibly blessed that there are men and women such as these who are prepared to lay down their lives for their country. And so from here in Boston where the first patriots stood up for freedom, I ask you as Americans, as Democrats, as modder -- modern day patriots, I ask you to stand up for our troops and give them that rousing round of applause that they so richly deserve. I stand before you this evening because I truly believe that no one will be more resolute in defending America nor in pursuing terrorists than John Kerry. And that no one will be more skilled in bringing allies back to our side. But I tell you, John Kerry cannot do it alone. You here tonight and all those that you represent have to be equally committed to give your total support to John Kerry and John Edwards as -- you have to give them that support and pledge it here tonight so that they can ensure that our armed forces remain the strongest, the best led, the best trained, and the best equipped in the world. You see, I believe in John Kerry. As a young man, he heeded his country's call to service when this country of ours needed him. He commanded in combat and did so with bravery and great distinction. And so he knows from experience a commander's responsibility to his troops. And so he stands with our troops and with their families. And that is why I stand with John Kerry. I thank you very much.

speaker: David Paterson
text: Thank you. As the first blind person to ever speak to The Democratic National Convention in history, it was only appropriate that I was brought on by a woman named Ann Sullivan. Well, I've been dreaming of this moment for 40 years. In harlem when I was 10 years old, I watched the 1964 Democratic national convention. Robert F. Kennedy offered us a picture of Democratic unity that inspired me to want to grow up and support candidates of a similar vision. You're seeing a dream come true. Today Americans are calling for an extraordinary man, a war hero and a peacetime leader. Who are they calling for? John Kerry! Americans are calling for a man who judges people on the content of their character, not by the color of their skin, who are Americans calling for? John Kerry! Americans are calling for a President Who understands that when children go hungry in America that it's really not America at all. Who are Americans calling for? John Kerry! Who are Americans calling for? John Kerry! And Americans are calling for a President Who can take this moment of decision and turn it into a date with destiny. Who are we calling for? John Kerry! I have a vision for New York state. I can't see it with my eyes, but I feel it in my heart. It's one where all new yorkers have good jobs and educations and health care. It's a vision of New York of growing prosperity, of balanced budgets and balanced priorities, upstate and down. It's a vision of New York, it's a vision of America that rejoices in its diversity, doesn't reject it. It's a New York of strong farmers and financiers, and New York city. We can't see this New York with our eyes yet, but we can feel it in our hearts. And if we work hard enough, we can build this New York a New York that will be so magnificent and make guestic that people won't need to see it -- majestic that people won't need to see it with their eyes because they will feel it in their hearts. And that, my fellow delegates, is John Kerry's view of America. It's the combination of what I seek for New York and what you envision where you live. In 2004 America stands at a moment of decision. And when we play our parts, fellow delegates, the 10-year-olds who watch this convention will remember that in 2004 that we did not succumb to the evil demons of our fears, but rather we embraced the better angels of our nature when we elected John Edwards and John Kerry to put America back on track! Thank you very much!

speaker: Dennis Kucinich
text: We Democrats in convention, united, we who built this country with the sweat of our brow, we the steelworkers, the autoworkers, the miners, the communication workers, the labors -- laborerers, the people who teach the children, who drive the trucks, who clean the streets, who hunger for justice, who nurse the sick, who represent the oppressed, who serve the meals, who stand at checkout counters, who build the bridges, who sleep under the bridges, who hunger for food, we who put out the fires, who police the streets, who protect this nation and the freedoms we celebrate tonight, the soldiers, the sailors, marines, and air force, we Democrats assembled, united for John Kerry, united to re-create our nation with the power of the ballot, to transform it with the power of the human heart and the power of the human spirit. Out of many we Democrats are one. We are left, right, center. We are one. We are black, white, red, brown, yellow. We are one. One for jobs and health care. One for peace and fair trade. One for our children's future. And we are one for John Kerry! We will carry America for Kerry and Kerry will carry America for us! We remember who we are. We are the party of the people. We are the party of f.d.r. And the new deal. The party of j.f.k. And the new frontier, of Lyndon Johnson and the great society, of Martin Luther King's dream, of Robert Kennedy's striving spirit, of cesar chavez, of Eleanor Roosevelt and human rights, infused with the passion of Paul wellstone from Minnesota. The humanity, the humanity of Jimmy Carter from plains, the engaging brilliance of Bill Clinton from hope. And we are the party of John Kerry, the next great Democratic President of the United States! The history of social and economic progress in America was written by the Democratic party. Democrats are the party of the minimum wage, the 40-hour week, time and a half for overtime. We are the party of the right to organize, the right to collective bargain, the right to strike, the right to a decent workplace, the right to a secure retirement. We are the party of workers' rights, civil rights, and women's rights. We are the party of national health care for senior citizens , of social security, public education, and rural electrification. When we show upholding the banner of social and economic justice, we win! And now we must create a new America. We must create a new America. In our national anthem when Francis Scott Key asked, does that star spangled banner yet wave oer the land of the free and the home of the brave, he connected freedom and bravery, democracy and courage, courage, America! Courage to replace this administration and once again honor our constitution and respect our bill of rights! Courage to reject doctrines which separate us from the world. Courage to stop the spread of nuclear weapons, biological and chemical weapons, land mines and small arms. Courage to work with the international criminal court, to reduce global warming and face seriously the challenge of global climate change. Courage, America! Courage to take the principles of nonviolence and make them part of the everyday life of our nation to work with the nations of the world to put an end to war. Courage, America, to create a government, to create a nation where our government achieves legitimacy, not from the money it spends on arms, but from the resources it channels into education, health care, job creation, housing, environmental protection, and sustainable energy policies. Courage to give John Kerry the chance to restart the 21st century. Courage to shake off this administration's deceptions, their attacks, and their fear mongering. Courage, America. This administration rushed us into a war based on distortions and misrepresentations. We must hold them accountable. Iraq had nothing to do with 9/11 or with Al Qaeda's role in 9/11. We have found no weapons of mass destruction in Iraq. I was Mayor Of Cleveland and I can tell you that I've seen weapons of mass destruction in our cities. Poverty is a weapon of mass destruction, joblessness is a weapon of mass destruction, homelessness, a weapon of mass destruction, racism, a weapon of mass destruction, fear, a weapon of mass destruction. We must disarm these weapons! We must disarm these weapons and renew our commitment to quality public schools and dedicated teachers and good housing and quality health care and decent jobs and stronger neighborhoods. It's been said, once we walk, there will be a path. So let us blaze a new path with John Kerry and John Edwards. This convention will lead us toward the victory not just of a party, but the victory of the American people over fear, a victory of hope over despair, a victory of faith over cynicism, a victory for health care for civil liberties, for workers' rights, for human rights, for the environment, for peace, courage, America! Courage, America! John Kerry, America! Thank you!

speaker: Diane Wilkerson
text: My fellow Democrats, I stand here today as a proud resident of Massachusetts ready to welcome those of you here and those watching in your homes to our home and the great city of Boston. Massachusetts Democrats are ready to stand shoulder to shoulder with all hard-working men and women to take our country back. And what better place to launch this revolution than the place where our democracy was born? As a seven-term state Senator From Boston, I have seen the harshest impact of this administration's policy. More Americans working without health insurance than four years ago. More people out of work. And many more without hope. I want a President Who believes that health care access ought to depend on whether you need it, not whether you can afford it. A President Who believes in public education and is willing to fund it. A President Who believes that women are smart enough to be charged with making decisions about our own bodies. And a President Who believes that civil rights are something to be celebrated and who would actually meet with the nation's civil rights leaders at least once in four years and be proud of it. A President Who would meet with organized labor and stand with ordinary people, not just in photo ops, but in their lives. And most importantly, I want a President Who would dig deep in his heart and soul before making the tough decision to send our sons and daughters off to war, who will restore a reputation for America that will allow us to travel abroad and again feel proud and fearless to call ourselves Americans. I believe that my senator, John F. Kerry, is the right man for the job. I was born in the state of Arkansas and my grandma was birth by a mid wife at a time when my mom wasn't welcome in a local hospital. I now reside in a great city of Boston. What an incredible journey it's been. From Arkansas which gave us our last great president, to Massachusetts which will give us our next. Join with me on the next leg of this journey as we send our Native son to Washington. Join with me as we work to Elect John F. Kerry the next President of the United States of America.

speaker: Dianne Feinstein
text: Thank you. Thank you very much. Thank you! Thank you very much! Thank you very much! Thank you very much! On behalf of Democrats all across this great nation, I am both proud and pleased to place the name of John Kerry in the nomination to become the 44th President of the United States of America! I have known John Kerry for more than a decade. I've worked with him in the Senate. I'm proud to call him a friend. John Kerry's a man who is skilled, capable, and experienced. He's a caring and compassionate person, a family man. In Vietnam as a naval officer, in the courtroom as a prosecutor, in the United States Senate representing the great state of Massachusetts, he has devoted his life to serving this nation. He understands the urgent need to bring this country together toward a common purpose, A United America, or as President Clinton so eloquently said last night, toward a more perfect union. John Kerry's chief goal as President Will be to move this nation in a new and different direction, away from the huge deficits and debts of the past four years, towards fiscal responsibility, away from the policies that eliminate good-paying American jobs and send those jobs overseas. For too many Americans, incomes are stagnant, jobs are threatened, workers work harder. But they earn less. This is not the American dream. There are families in my state of California, 6.6 million californians who have no health care coverage, not even insurance for catastrophic illness. John Kerry's health care proposals will change this. America can do better. And with John Kerry, America will do better. As president, John Kerry will build those bridges necessary to restore America's credibility abroad and he'll build the coalitions and the alliances necessary to win the war on terror. As the member of the Senate intelligence committee, I know what we need to make changes in our intelligence community. That's the conclusion of the joint house intelligence investive in the 9/11 and it's the conclusion of the 9/11 commission. But 9/11 happened three years ago, and those changes have not been made. I can tell you John Kerry won't wait. And America will be safer. John Kerry will work his heart out to see that our children are well educated and well prepared to meet the challenges of the future, will work to free this nation from oil dependence on foreign oil and to keep it clean and green and most importantly, he'll lead the way to restore hope, opportunity and equality for all our citizens. This members' election for me is the most important election of my lifetime, for we face unprecedented threats at home and abroad. My friends, this election is the test of our time, for no less is at stake than the character of our nation. But we've got a strong, new leader, prepared and ready to confront these challenges and lead this nation to a better tomorrow. That's why I'm so proud to place in the nomination John Kerry to be the 44th President of these United States! Thank you very much. Thank you.

speaker: Donna Christano Da Mattason
text: My fellow Democrats, is this a big ten party or what? As a representative from one of our nation's most beautiful and proud offshore areas, the U.S.. Virgin islands and as a representative from a region the caribbean, that is home to our close and loyal neighbors, I cannot tell you how proud I am to stand before you and speak at this grand convention. This is a historic moment. No person from a territory and no fwrun a region has -- my region has ever addressed a major national party convention. My presence here is proof of the one America that we can and we will build. The one America that is secure for everyone. A nation where there are no disparities in health care, health status, and health coverage. And where those who live in offshore areas have the cap on Medicaid lifted and s.s.I. Benefits extended. A nation where the public health system is intact and completely prepared throughout our land. A nation where all of our children have an opportunity to learn and grow to their fullest potential. No matter where they're from, what language they speak, or the color of their skin. A nation where every right is protected and where all Americans in the states or its offshore areas can vote as every other American does for our commander in chief. A nation where we are all secure from the smallest rural community to the most remote Indian reservation. From our nation's eastern most point at st. Cr oirks x to the western most point of the same name on the pacific island of guam. We are here this week because we share a hope. It is the hope that inspired my ancestors as they held fast to the dream of freedom. It is the hope that drove generations of immigrants to leave all that they knew behind them and to seek a better life in a new land. It is the hope that we can all share in the promise of America. So let's help John Kerry and John Edwards keep America safe. And restore hope and optimism in our country. Together we can make America the great nation we are destined to be. Thank you my fellow Democrats. God bless.

speaker: Ed Pastor
text: Muchas gracias. Buenos noches. I am deeply honored to be part of this historic moment. Not only are we nominating John Kerry and Zwrode lead our party at this critical moment in our nation's history, but we are now energized as a party and a people to make honor and opportunity the hallmarks of America once again. That is, indeed, something worth celebrating. I represent Arizona. The great city of phoenix. And I can tell you that my community, my community is ready for new leadership. Leadership that will make America tronger at home and respected in the world. That leadership is right here. Our next Vice President John Edwards, our next President of the United States John Kerry. With their leadership we will create good-paying jobs here at home so we can export products to the rest of the world. And we will not export jobs. We will lower health care and prescription drug costs and expand health care coverage to relee the great burden of these costs. We will once again get serious about improving our schools to make sure that every child has a productive life. We will also improve job training and retraining to make sure that our American workers adapt to a changing economy. We will harness American ingenuity to make us independent of middle east oil. So never again an American will have to go to war for oil. All my life, beginning in a small mining town in Arizona, claypool, where I was born, I have witnessed time and time again, the great potential of the human spirit to create a better life for all of us. It is a potential constantly being expanded by the immigrant families like mine who came to America to work and to achieve the American dream. What the American people need to succeed today are pro-job and pro-growth policies. That will give us quality education, affordable health care, will secure our energy and give us a booming economy. And that John Edwards, John Kerry, are eddie to begin on January 2005. -- ared arey to begin on January 2005. Let's pick up these tools and go to work. Ladies and gentlemen, let's change the direction of this country. Let's get to work and Elect The next President of the United States, John Kerry.

speaker: Ed Rendell
text: Thank you. You know, I thought terry mckickoff leadoff liked Bob Graham and liked me but putting us after Al Sharpton, I don't know. It is important to talk about a subject tonight, energy independence. The greatest of the American people is we are at our best in time os of crisis. In 1945 America faced a europe with a shattered infrastructure, a decimated economy, and threatened by an encroaching Soviet union. To meet that challenge we launched the marshal plan, formed NATO, and changed the course of history forever. Today the crisis is on our shores. The challenge before us is here at home. The price of gas at the pump is at record levels. The cost of energy to run our factories is wreaking havoc on our industrial competitiveness and families are finding it harder and harder to afford to heat their homes. But instead of responding with big ideas that put Americans back to work, we are exporting jobs, dollars, and worst of all opportunity. The opportunity to strengthen our economy by becoming the world's leader in new environmental technology. Clean cars, efficient appliances and alternate fuels that are the wave of the future. And more than that because we lack a sound energy policy, we are forced to deal with tyrants who fund terrorists in order to keep the lights on in our homes and fuel our cars. Our foreign policy is skewed. Our values are compromised. And we are paying a price. John Kerry said it best when he said, no young American soldier should fight and die because of our dependence on foreign oil. We are losing jobs, and middle class families are feeling the squeeze all because America is too dependent on foreign oil. Oil that flows on the rim of world events and too frauven the wells of dictators. John Kerry knows to build a stronger America we must do better. We can no longer rely on foreign oil fields to power our economy. We can no longer compromise our security to keep the pipelines from far away kingdoms flowing freely. That is why, tonight, near the birthplace of the American revolution, we must declare our independence again. Our energy independence. We must select a President To lead us towards a safer, stronger America. An America free from the tyranny of foreign oil. We must Elect John Kerry. And America should listen clearly. John Kerry has a plan to move America away from its dependence on mideast oil. First, to bring down our fuel costs he will do whatever it takes to deliver clean, natural gas to our factories' doors in abundant supply and reasonable prices. And John Kerry will roll up his sleeves to work with coal companies to scrub their plants and to help generate clean coal energy to heat our homes. In Pennsylvania we're already using waste coal to generate lots of electricity and coal mine gas is being converted into usable energy. And when John Kerry is president, we will become the world leader in the clean coal economy. And not just in Pennsylvania. But in Kentucky, in Illinois, in wyoming, and, yes, in West Virginia. Second, John Kerry knows that moving towards energy independence means creating more jobs. There are thousands of people who are choosing to drive the new hybrid cars that, as we all know, get terrific gas mileage. Yet the waiting lists stretch for six months with places on those waiting lists being augsed off on ebay for -- audition off on ebay for $1,000. Where are those cars being snead Japan. To ensure those jobs are here John Kerry will create $10 billion for car manufactures to convert factories to build these cars right here in the good old U.S..a. And he'll provide tax credits to you, to you, to you, and other consumers so they can purchase those vehicles. Third, John Kerry will put America ahead of the rest of the world by boosting demand for alternative sources of energy to 20%. To meet that demand and put more Americans at work, he intends to make America the largest producer of alternative fuels. Now, I don't have a ph.d. In energy policy, but I do know this. If I use less energy in my house, my electric bill goes down. It's common sense. Common sense for me. And common sense for America. And that's why John Kerry will cut the federal energy bill by $14 billion. And that will free up those resources for us to invest them in education and in our economy. And he will help every American family in business to do the same thing, driving down their energy costs as well. 228 years ago in my hometown of Philadelphia a Native son of Boston John Hancock signed his name to the declaration of independence knowing full well the risk of taking this action. There is only one candidate for president. Only one. Who will courageously affix his signature to America's new declaration of energy inddepens because he knows full well the risk of not doing so. And the name of that candidate, the name of the next President of the United States, John Kerry! Thank you.

speaker: Eimy Santiago
text: Thank you. Buenos noches. Good evening. My name is Eimy santiago. I'm 21 years old. And I want to tell you how John Kerry helped save my life. From this day to my street of Springfield, Massachusetts, it's only a 90-minute drive by a world away. Growing up, I didn't always make the right choices. When I was 14, I ran away from home and into a world of trouble. I fell into a bad crowd and sold illicit drugs. I was trapped in an abusive relationship and when I got pregnant, I dropped out of high school. A lot of people gave up on me and for a while, I even gave up on myself. That's when a program called youthbuild changed my life. And youthbuild has had no greater champion than Senator John Kerry. Because of John Kerry, a small-state effort has become a national program focused on education and community service. Because of John Kerry, thousands of young people like me have been given a second chance to earn their g.e.d. Because of John Kerry, we have learned jobs and construction by building affordable housing for the homeless and thousands of low-income families. And because of John Kerry, I started to believe in myself again. He even came to my class in Springfield once. There I was, a high school dropout and seven months pregnant. And John Kerry looked at me and he listened to me. He shook my hand and he said, you can make it. And today I am making it. Thanks to youth build I earned my g.e.d. I enrolled in my local community college. It hasn't been easy. My little girl, Alex, is now 3 years old and stays with day care and my family. I work full time and in the afternoons and at night. I go to school full time in the mornings and I'm proud of my grades, a 3. 0 average. Across America today there are millions of young feel like me trying to make something of ourselves. Studying hard and working hard. And I think it is time we had a President That worked just as hard for us. America deserves a President Who means it when we talk about leafing no child behind. John Kerry thinks it isen acceptable that nearly half of Hispanics, African-American and Native American youth do not graduate from high school. Under President Kerry no quality of a child's education will not depend on the wealth of that child's neighborhood. Education will be funded, classes will be smaller and teachers will be paid better. And America deserves a President Who is committed to helping students afford a college education. Under President Kerry, young people will be able to pay for four years of tuition at a public university in return for serving their communities. And working students, like me, will get the training and technical skills we need for today's workforce. Tomorrow we'll go back to Springfield, back to my job and in the fall back to school. Next spring I hope to graduate with an associate's degree in human services. For a career I want to help other young people turn their lyes around and for the future I want to give my little girl, Alex, a better life. Now because of a Senator Named John Kerry believed in a young woman like me. That is the difference he made in my life. Just imagine what difference John Kerry will make for America. I cannot think of a better way to thank him than to nominate John Kerry for the President of the United States of America. Vote for John Kerry. Thank you.

speaker: Elijah Cummings
text: Maryland. I am Elijah cummings, the son of Ruth and Robert Cummings who began their lives as share croppers on the same land where their foreparents once worked as slaves. They never lost their faith in God, in themselves, nor in the vision that some day America would tear down the walls of injustice and embrace them as full citizens. In search of a better life for their family, my parents moved from South Carolina to a small house in south baltimore in 1944. They worked hard every day. They taught their seven children the American vision of one nation under God, indivisible, with liberty and justice for all. They joined a labor union to obtain fair wages, health insurance, and the overtime pay that would allow them to buy their first house. They made certain that their children attended school every day and lived to see all of us succeed. My parents lived the American dream. The same dream that we as Democrats have always worked for to make possible for all Americans. Tonight I am tremendously honored to stand before you as the Chairman Of the congressional black caucus, 39 courageous women and men who many call the conscience of The Congress of the United States. We collectively represent over 26 million Americans of every background, faith, tradition, and race. These Americans, many of whom have made the same journey as my parents, have sent me to Boston with this message -- we are living in difficult times, but difficulty and danger are nothing new to Americans. We are a people of tremendous courage and unbelievable faith. The bible teaches us that God has not given us the spirit not of fear but of power and of love and of a sound mind! And in this spirit of courage, reason, compassion, and strength, we are doing everything in our power to protect our nation. We are supporting our troops and those who protect us here at home. And we are committed to restoring America's standing in the world. As Americans, though, we will not live in a spirit of fear. And we will be strong in defending our freedom and our civil liberties. And I have a special message for anyone who might try to keep us from the voting booth this year, we will exercise our constitutional right to vote! We are determined to ensure the integrity of our voting system. And this time, this time all of our votes will be counted! Finally, we will keep marching forward on history's road towards universal civil rights. Ladies and gentlemen, terrorists are not the only threat that we face. For tens of millions of Americans, the most imminent threat to their lives is our failure to provide quality health care for all. And the greatest long-term threat to our nation and our national security is our failure to properly educate all of our children! America, America can and will overcome all of these challenges if we are strong and if we are unified. The foundation of our strength depends on creating good jobs and economic opportunity for all. Our national unity demands tax reforms that truly reflect shared sacrifice. Our nation, our nation deserves more than political slogans and sound bites. We must continue to fight for those issues that touch the center of people's lives every day! And that is centered on firm and fearless leadership that President John Kerry and Vice President John Edwards and a Democratic Congress will provide! Ladies and gentlemen, this is the most important election of our lives! And we as Democrats pledge to America tonight that we will synchronize our nation's conduct with o conscience. We will Elect John Kerry President And John Edwards Vice President And we will build a bridge to an America that is strong, free, and unified. May God bless you and may God bless America!

speaker: Elizabeth Edwards
text: Thank you. I'm Elizabeth Edwards and tonight I am the very proud mother of cate Edwards. John and I have been truly blessed with a beautiful and strong family. And we're blessed, too, by you, our great Democratic family. You have no idea how great you all look from right here. This has been quite a year for John and for me. We started last January crisscrossing America, talking about how with determination and vision and optimism we can end the injustice of two americas. And this January is going to end when we move two great friends, two great Americans, John Kerry and Teresa Heinz Kerry into 1600 pennsylvania avenue. Teresa represents the victory of spirit and will over tyranny and tragedy. And she will be the most generous first lady in the history of this country. I am so honored to stand with her. John Kerry was in the Navy and so was my father. I grew up traveling around the world, living on Navy bases, but I always knew I was home when I saw the American flag. Like John Kerry, my father fought for this country. Like John Kerry, he was decorated, risking his life in her service. My father had another thing in common with John Kerry, and with so many of the uniformed men and women across this country and around the world, he has the right stuff. I married a man with the right stuff, too. He found his own way to serve in his community he was the driving force behind two after-school centers that meet the needs of young people. He was santa claus to needy families. And when that wasn't enough, to an entire inner city childcare center. You know, he even looks dashing in a santa suit. In youth activities he did what so many Americans do, giving up their weekends and evenings to coach young people in basketball and soccer, in his church in urban ministries and prayer groups and for 20 years in his work, fighting for those who could not fight for themselves. But never -- it never mattered how powerful the opponent, it never mattered how entrenched the interests, if the cause was just and his voice was needed, he was there. And now he serves the great state of North Carolina and The United States Senate, still fighting for those who count on him to be their voice. Using his intellect and his eloquence, he has fought to improve our health care choices and to protect our environment. And he called attention to the threat of terrorism before September 11. You know, I married the smartest, toughest, sweetest man I know. And in two days we will celebrate 27 years of marriage. The way we always do. We'll do it the way we always do, at Wendy's. Whether it's Wendy's or Washington, I found that it's true, it's not where you go, it's who you go with. But none of the things -- none of the things I've mentioned are the reason that I married John Edwards. I married him because he was the single most optimistic person that I have ever known. He knew there was a brighter day ahead even as he swept the floors of the Cotton mill as a high school student. He knew if he worked hard enough he could be the first in his family to go to college. He knew that he could outwork and outtough any battalion of lawyers to find justice. And he continued that fight in Washington. Courageously, eloquently, with one simple goal, to make the opportunities of America available to all Americans. We deserve leaders who allow their faith and moral core, our faiths and moral core to draw us closer together, not drive us farther apart. We deserve leaders who believe in each of us and fight for all of us. My rock, my love, and your next Vice President, John Edwards.

speaker: Frank Lautenberg
text: First, hello New Jersey. Ladies and gentlemen, I'm here to discuss a very familiar subject. And that is John Kerry's strong support for the state of Israel. Now, I have visited Israel over 80 times. I served as the national Chairman Of The United Jewish Appeal. I know the strategic, political, and cultural bonds between The United States and Israel. And I'm also very close to my Senate colleague John Kerry with whom I have worked for 20 years. Now, I known John Kerry. John Kerry is a friend of mine. And John Kerry is a great friend of Israel. When John Kerry was putting his life on the line to fight for our country in Vietnam, he learned the value of teamwork among his crowe mates and to fight -- crewmates and to fight the enemy. Now when our nation faces the threat of terror, John Kerry is ready to lead an all-out effort to twin war. In our fight against terrorism John Kerry knows one of America's most important crewmates is Israel. When it was time to stand up for Israel and fight for the United States, John Kerry was not just standing there. He was leading. And I'll tell you this, John Kerry will face up to those who try to blame Israel for the turmoil and the violence in the middle east. Israel has become the scapegoat for despots and corrupt governments in the region. A pir -- pirria. With John Kerry Israel will have a strong voice because he will never stop reminding the world of Israel's respect for life and democracy. His desire to make peace. And John Kerry will never sit silently in the face of hateful anti-semitic speech at home or abroad. John Kerry defends Israel's right to build a barrier. He agrees the fence is a legitimate response to the terror Israel faces. It is an effective tool against terror. It is not a matter to be litigated in front of the internal court of justice or The United Nations General Assembly. That has done nothing to address the terrorist threat that Israel faces. As president, John Kerry's commitment to a safe and secure Jewish state will be unwaivering. It is a pledge he has always kept in the United States Senate, whether through his votes on economic aid, military security or the location of the U.S.. Embassy. It is one he will always keep as president. John Kerry knows Israel is an indy spenceable friend and that I -- ind spenceable friend and that I whyly tell you that Israel will never have a better friend in the White House than John Kerry. We must do it for America and the Democratic world. Thank you all very, very much.

speaker: Gregory Meeks
text: New York! 18 months ago I sought a candidate who could unite our party and unite America. And because my southeastern queens district is in the city that was attacked on 9/11, I wanted a candidate who knew how to protect America. I wanted a candidate who would wage a winning war against terrorism, a candidate who would make America safe, not just safer. I say to you tonight that John Kerry is that candidate. He is what America needs, a new direction in policy and new leadership in the White House. I've seen his strength of character, judgment, and conviction in action.
