I proudly second the nomination of Senator John Kerry as the next the President of the United States of America! I am also proud to know Senator John Edwards. These two men who I've welcomed to my city, worshiped with us in Columbus churches, walked through our neighborhoods so that they could see for themselves the true spirit of our city and our state. I do not take this responsibility lightly because tonight I dedicate this nomination to the many sons and daughters serving in the United States armed forces, serving right here at home, serving overseas in Iraq or in Afghanistan. And I dedicate this nomination to my youngest son, lance corporal John David Coleman, a United States marine, training in Israel today. John Kerry, I trust you to lead him as he performs his duty, and I pray that one day we can bring all America's sons and daughters home safe and sound to a better America, a united America. God bless you and may God bless America! Ohio!

speaker: Mike Flaherty
text: Thank you. My fellow Democrats, my name is Michael Flaherty. I'm the President of the Boston City Council. I'm also a delegate to this convention from the great ninth congressional district of Massachusetts. Tonight I thank the city and the people of Boston for being such gracious hosts for this convention. There are lots of reasons to love Boston, and I hope you've found that out this week. We have lively politics, great sports teams, vibrant and diverse neighborhoods, and some of the greatest and hardest working people you'll ever meet. I was born in a public housing project right here in south Boston. My parents struggled to raise and educate me well. I worked hard as a proud member of teamsters local 25 to make my way through college and law school. Today my wife, loreen, and I are raising our own children. We bought a home in the same neighborhood where we grew up. I am living the American dream. But I worry that the American dream may not be there for my daughter, ella, and my sons patrick, Michael, and Jack. Today I worry about what the future holds for them. The American dream must be possible for everyone. This is the promise of The Democratic Party. It's why I'm a Democrat and it's why I ran for public office. It's Democrats who understand the real problems of American families and it's Democrats who find the solutions. I want a future where we help parents raise their kids, where all Americans get the education and training they need, where our streets are safer, where we treat substance abusers, where we protect and create jobs for working men and women. That is why I want John Kerry to be our next president. I believe that we can put our country on a new course. I believe we can bring change and progress to America. I know some people have given up hope. But we have an opportunity to restore hope in 2004. Some don't think we can do it. Some don't think that we can keep the dream alive. But I'll tell you, I'm a Boston red sox fan, and I'll never stop believing. Thank you all for your time and attention. Good luck, John Kerry. God bless you all and God bless America!

speaker: Patrick Leahy
text: Thank you. Thank you. I'm Patrick Leahy and I'm from Vermont. The Vice President Doesn't always agree with me and so if the Vice President Is watching this it may be time for him to change channels and turn off his TV set. And apparently he turned off the teleprompter, too. Like millions of Americans I'm dismayed by the direction the current administration is leading America both at home and abroad. In an outpouring of internal sympathy following the attacks of September 11, thousands of people played the star spangled banner, NATO stood with us, a newspaper in France declared, "we are all Americans." sadly, three short years later, most of that goodwill is gone. The result of a go-it-alone administration that has pulled The United States out of treat eye after treaty, they have alienated our allies, they have undermined our national security with a misguided rush to invade Iraq. And just last week the 9/11 commission laid down a blueprint for a stronger and a wiser, a more effective war on terrorism. They said America needs to use all the tools in its tool box to win back the world's hearts and minds and get the cooperation we need. I agree with these findings. But I also believe America needs something else. America needs a new president. A new President Who has the vision and the credibility to repair the damage that the current administration has done to America's reputation. A new President Who understands an America can't just tell other nations what to do, but we must also inspire them to do it. A new president, a new President Who will once again restore wisdom and judgment to The White House. A new President Named John Kerry. Now, let me -- I want to share some common sense that any of my fellow vermonters will tell you. If you hire a carpenter if you hire a carpenter who only sees the hammer in his tool box, every problem looks like a nail. It is time for America to put a new President On the job. John Kerry who will use every tool at America's disposal, not just a hammer, to build a stronger and safer America at home and abroad. So this November -- this November I invite you to join me and millions of our flole citizens in building that better -- our fellow citizens in building that better America and cast our votes for the next President of the United States, John Kerry. God bless you. Thank you. And God bless our wonderful, wonderful country.

speaker: Pueblo Asandia
text: Good afternoon and greetings from New Mexico and the Pueblo pueblosandia. I' am one of the governors of New Mexico. Ours is the only state with 20 governors one for each of the 19pueblos and one for the state. Despite overwelming numbers, somehow Governor Bill Richardson gets a little bit more media attention than the rest of us combined. Never the less, we love Governor Richardson in the state of New Mexico. I am extremely proud to be here on behalf of my people, Indian nations and Native Americans throughout New Mexico and The United States. While in the past, a Native American vote may have been overlooked, we understand the importance of participating in the political process. Like bill, senators Kerry and Edwards support tribal sovereignty. In fact, Native Americans make up the swing vote in many battleground states like New Mexico. In New Mexico, our tribes have helped Elect Bill. He reflects New Mexico's rich cultural diversity and has brought a new energy to our state. We look forward to having that same kind of national leadership in an administration of Senator Kerry and Edwards. for the people of my Pueblo, as for all Americans, three issues we face are health care, education and the environment. The Kerry Edwards health plan recognizes that minorities suffer more than others from a variety of illnesses. Native American communities, alcoholism, diabetes are at epidemic levels. The crisis in health care is one war that's worth fighting. As regards to education, Kerry Edwards' plan is more than an empty promise to leave no child behind. It will provide resources to expand early childhood and after-school programs, raise high school graduation rates and give all high school graduates an opportunity to attend college college. Finally, our environment should not be compromised to benefit special interests. . >> Native Americans have always known the importance of living in harmony with our natural world. History teaches us that policies affecting our lands and natural resources must be balanced. Beautiful or unique places must be preserved for future generations to enjoy. Happy labels like the healthy force initiative should not disguise policies which really mean no trees left behind. Thank you and I look forward to working with all of you in electing senators Kerry and Edwards to the Vice President And presidenty President President of America. God bless.

speaker: Robert Kennedy Jr.
text: For 20 years as an environmental advocate, I've been disciplined about being bipartisan and nonpartisan in my approach to these issues. There is no such thing as Republican children or Democratic children. And the worst thing that can happen to the environment is if it becomes the province of a single party or a bipartisan issue. And all the national environmental leaders on the environmental movement agree with me. And if five years ago you asked the leaders of all the major groups in this country, what is the grafest threat to the global -- gravest threat to the global environment, they would have told you habitat instruction, global warming, today they will give you one answer unanimously, it is George W. Bush. We have never had a President In this country who has made this kind of direct assault on all of our environmental laws. In 3 1/2 years he has promoted 400 major environmental rollbacks designed to eviscerate 30 years of environmental law. And he's put polluters in charge of all the regulatory agencies that are supposed to protect Americans from pollution. The second in command of e.p.a. Is a monsanto lobbyist, the head of the air division is a utility lobbyist, the head of the forest service of the United States is a timber industry lobbyist, the head of public lands is a mining industry lobbyist. And if you ask the people and these people are not answering government service for the public interest, they are entering government service to subvert the very laws that they are supposed to enforce. And if you ask Dick Cheney or George Bush, why are you doing this, what they will say to you is we have to choose between environmental protection and economic prosperity. And John Kerry knows that is a false choice. John Kerry knows that good environmental policy 100% of the time is identical to good economic policy. If we want to measure our economy, and this is how we ought to be measuring it based upon how it produces jobs and the dignity of jobs over the generations, over the long term and how it preserves the value of the assets of our communities. If on the other hand we want to do what Dick Cheney and George Bush are urging us to do, which is treat the planet as if it were business in liquidation, to convert our natural resources to cash as quickly as possible, to have a few years of pollution-based prosperity, we can generate an instantaneous cash flow and the illusion of a prosperous economy, but our children will pay for our joyride and pay for it with diluted landscapes and poor health and huge cleanup costs that will amplify over times and won't be able to pay. Deficit spending is a way of overloading the costs of our generation's prosperity on to the back of our children. And this is the government of deficit spending. We all know how they turned a $5 trillion surplus into a $5 trillion deficit, shifting investment in our community into the pockets of the wealthiest Americans and corporations. And we all know how they eroded and destroyed all of the goodwill and the moral authority that our nation once wielded around the world. And I remember when I was a young boy and I ran around with my uncle, President Kennedy, and with my father, Robert Kennedy, and we visited all the countries of europe and saw the vast numbers of people who looked to us for moral leadership and the Europeans who proudly named their streets after our presidents George Washington, Teddy Roosevelt, Franklin Roosevelt, John Kennedy. And it took us 350 years to build up those reservoirs of moral authority and be beloved around the world. And in 3 1/2 years, this President Has destroyed that inment -- that investment. But the most destructive policies of this administration are those that have been directed towards our environment, our arrow -- that now threaten our air, our water, our public lands, the source of our values and virtues and character as a people that give context to our communities and that make us proud to be Americans. John Kerry understands that we are not protecting the environment for the sake of the fishes and the birds. We're protecting it for our sake because we understand it's the infrastructure of our communities. And if we want to meet our obligations as a generation, as a civilization, as a nation, which is to create communities for our children, that provide them the same opportunities for dignity and enrichment as the communities that our parents gave us, we've got to start by protecting our environmental infrastructure. John Kerry understands that we're protecting -- that nature enriches us, it enriches us economically, yes, but enriches us aesthetically and recreationally and historically and spiritually. And human beings of other an tights besides money. And if we don't feed them we're not going to grow up and become the kind of beings and not the kind of nation that our creator intended us to become. Nature enriches us. When we destroy it, we diminish ourselves and impoverish our children. John Kerry has been the best champion of the environment for 20 years on Capitol Hill. And he is the man we must send to The White House if we're going to preserve the dignity of this nation and live up to the promises of our history and of our culture and our citizens. Thank you very much.

speaker: Steve Brozak
text: Good evening. My name is Steve Brozak. I'm a husband and a father of two young daughters. My wife and I are the children of immigrant parents. I'm a businessperson who co-founded a small investment banking firm. And I'm also proud to say I am a United States marine! I rose to the rank of Lieutenant Colonel. And after the attacks of September 11, I volunteered to go back on active cute. -- active duty. And today I am running to represent my home state of New Jersey in the United States Congress! If you told me 18 months ago that I would end up addressing the Democratic convention, I would have said you are crazy. You see, up until then I was a Republican, but not anymore! On my last tour of duty, my job was to help reservists who came home from Iraq, Afghanistan, and places all over the world to help them get back on their feet, to help them with their jobs and their families. I saw their struggle and their hardships. I saw how their families coped with deployments that kept being extended and children who were growing up without their moms and dads. When I was deployed to the middle east, I saw what our troops are facing there. How our military is so overextended, that tours of duty last far longer than what soldiers had originally signed up for, how hard it is for our soldiers to complete their mission without the proper equipment to keep them safe. I saw how hard it is for them to shoulder this burden alone without our allies. And I saw how hard it is for our soldiers to know, while they wear their uniforms with pride, the world looks at them with suspicion. So a year and a half ago, while back home, I went to my local town hall and I switched parties. I became a Democrat! You see, I didn't change my belief in what makes a patriot never changed. My belief in a national security policy that makes us truly safer didn't change. And my belief that you only send Americans into a war with honest purpose and plans for peace didn't change. What changed was the direction of the Republican party. So I did the only honorable thing and I changed parties and I became a Democrat! I have never considered myself to be a very partisan person, but the Republican party left me behind, and so I had no choice but to leave the Republican party behind. But I am far from alone. And I will be in the majority come November 2! It's not just Democrats, it's Republicans and independents, it's Americans from all walks of life who believe that to keep America safe, to defeat Al Qaeda, we need both smart bombs and wise policy that America must be strong at home, strong abroad, and respected in the world. John Kerry understands this. He learned that lesson the hard way, as a Lieutenant Commanding a swift boat in the deltas of Vietnam, as President John Kerry will honor the service and the sacrifice of our men and women in uniform by keeping the military strong and ready to respond to any threats we may face. John Kerry will be a commander in chief who will not hesitate for one moment to use the military might of the United States to destroy the terrorists who threaten our way of life. John Kerry is a leader. And this marine will proudly follow John Kerry into battle! John Kerry has a plan to win the war on terror. John Kerry will strengthen our alliances and restore America's special place in the world as a nation to be admired rather than questioned. John Kerry will give our men and women who serve in uniform solid support instead of hollow rhetoric. John Kerry is someone who means what he says. And John Kerry will make sure that our veterans are shown the full gratitude and appreciation of our nation! My friends, Iraq is filled with professional soldiers, patriotic reservists and guard members who long for a mission that is planned and purposesful and are long past ready to be reunited with their families. One of these men is a friend of mine from New Jersey who looked after my wife and family while I was on active duty last year. This man, a veteran in his mid 40's is now being sent straight into the battle zone in Iraq with no promise of returning home to his family before 2006. As my friend is put into harm's way, I will repay my debt to him and look after his family. But I will do something else, I will fight alongside John Kerry to create a better, stronger America, united at home and respected in the world! If you want to support more troops, if you want to help their families, if you want to build a nation that is the envy and not the enemy of the world, I ask you to join us tonight in this fight, help make John Kerry the President of the United States! My fellow Americans this election is not about party labels or partisan bickering, this election is about creating the America that we learned about in school, the America that brought my immigrant parents to this shore, the America that I fought to defend, an America where freedom is cherished, an America where individuals are respected, an America where anything is possible because each of us has the right to go as far as our minds can imagine and our hearts can dream. Let's build that America, a safer, stronger, more respected America! Let's make John Kerry the next the President of the United States! Thank you and good night.

speaker: Steve Wesley
text: Fellow delegates, greetings from the great state of California. Home to the engine of our country's economy, silicon valley, and home to two great Democratic women senators, Diana Feinstein and Barbara Boxer. And on November 2, home to 55 55 electricelectoral votes for John Kerry. Our opponents are tough and our fight will not be easy. But John Kerry knows how to win tough fights. By inspiring people with bold ideas. California itself is an idea. It's our nation's constant reminder that we are a land of opportunity for all Americans. To win in November, we must must be the party of opportunity. We must be the party that opens doors no matter where you're from, what you look like, or who you love. This is personal to me. My wife is a Chinese immigrant who grew up on public assistance, but in the course of one generation became a highly successful technology executive. Ta's the American dream our -- that's the American dream our pleart always stand for. To -- our party will always stand for. To win in November we must be the party of jobs, jobs in technology, STEM cell research, health care. Seven years ago I took a job in a small startup. I was the 22nd employee and sat in a lawn chair at a small cubicle. Today that company has over 7,000 employees. And a market value larger than general motors. It's called ebay. And that's the kind of company and the kind of jobs our country needs. And John Kerry knows how to create them. Finally, to win in November, we must be the party of optimism. A truman Democrat once said of all the forces that make for a better world, none is so indispensable, none so powerful as hope. We must heed that call and be the party of hope. We must heed it because our opponent cans not. They say -- our opponents cannot. They say maybe one day we will rebuild our cities and schools, maybe one day tax breaks for the few will benefit the many. John Kerry says maybe one day is one day too many. John Kerry says we can have good jobs today. We can invest in our children today. And we can build a tronger America today. And on November 2, we will have that day when we Elect The next President of the United States John Kerry! Thank you.

speaker: Tom Allen
text: Listen to people in maine. They say to me in increasingly anxious and insistent voices: we must have help to provide our families and employees with health insurance. We cannot afford the health care we need. Sometimes it's the voice of a husband with no insurance, two children, and a wife just diagnosed with multiple sclerosis. Sometimes it's the voice of a small businesswoman with 10 employees who wants to hire quality workers but cannot, because she can't afford to offer health coverage. Sometimes it's the voice of a laid-off 60-year-old man who cannot find a job with health coverage and doesn't yet qualify for Medicare. for the last four years, their voices, their pleas for help, have not been heard in the White House. But John Kerry hears your voices voices. And I say today: help is on the way. John Kerry believes that a stronger, more secure America is, is one with affordable health care for all. John Kerry will put the health of America's families ahead of insurance and farm siewrt cal industry profits. John Kerry will reign in the exploding cost of prescription drugs so those who barely make ends meet won't have to choose between food and life-saving medications. You know, American businesses need decent affordable health coverage. Millions of workers who lost good-paying jobs with good benefits are now stuck in low-wage jobs without health insurance. John Kerry hears your voices and help is on the way. Catastrophic medical expenses strike only a small fraction of Americans each year, but they force up insurance rates for everyone. John Kerry will cut insurance premiums up to $1,000 by taking away the burden of the most expensive cases from employers, freeing them to offer higher quality care to more workers at a lower cost. Health care costs are also blunting the competitive edge of American entrepreneurs from the auto inds tree to internet startups. The "detroit news" just reported that health care costs add $1,000 to the price of every Ford vehicle built in America. So instead of exporting more American goods, we are shipping millions of American jobs overseas where health care costs a fraction of what we must pay here. John Kerry and John Edwards hear your voices. By giving every American access to quality, affordable health care, they will create a more competitive, a stronger, and a more secure America. Thank you.

speaker: Tom Reilly
text: I am privileged to say a few words to you, the delegates, who are the heart and soul of our Democratic party. For 25 years I have known and I have worked with John Kerry. For 25 years I have been proud to call John Kerry my friend. As a young prosecutor in middlesex, John Kerry was already a leader. He came to the office and said we can do better and we did. He recruited our states's most committed and best prosecutors. He reorganized the office and he turned the district attorney's office into the best one in Massachusetts, if not the nation. John Kerry didn't just talk, he acted. And the John Kerry that I know will do the same in the White House. President Kerry will stand up and make sure that every child gets a quality education and can afford to go to college. He'll stand up and make sure that every American has access to affordable quality health care. President Kerry will stand up for our seniors and make sure that they are protected and have the quality of life they so richly deserve. And President Kerry will stand up and protect our environment and make sure that the clean air act is fully enforced. Over the past four years we have been on the wrong course, a course that ignores the dreams and aspirations of regular people and families. This week, here in my hometown of Boston, by Boston harbor, we began charting a new course for the American people, a new course that includes all of our people, a new course that brings people together to solve problems for the common good, a new course that establishes America's role in the world as a moral, fair, and powerful leader. My friends, less than a mile from this center stands a monument on bunker hill. It is a monument to the first major battle that led to the birth of this proud nation. Today at this convention we are witnessing the rebirth of A United Democratic Party. A Democratic party that will work tirelessly and with purpose for all Americans. And in November this great country will Elect John Kerry, the next President of the United States! God bless you! Do bless America! Thank you! Thank you!

speaker: Veronica Garcia
text: Good evening. Greetings from New Mexico. The great land of enchantment. Buenas noches. Our country has always provided an opportunity for citizens to achieve their dreams. I am living proof that this is true. Growing up in albuquerque, New Mexico, I'm the first of my family to graduate from high school. A cycle of poverty was broken when I went on to finish my university studies. My dreams became a reality because I was fortunate to have access to a quality public education. Today this is not true for all children in America. I know. I am a life-long educator. I've been in the trefrpbls as a teacher, a principal, and a superintendent. I believe that the Kerry-Edwards ticket represents strong values and commitment. I support John Kerry and John Edwards because they have demonstrated that they value education as the path to opportunity for all Americans. They will do this by first ensuring no child is left behind by fully funding this reform and not shortchanging it by $27 billion. Second, promising that no child leffed behind moves from an underfunded program to a fully funded reform effort that will help all schools excel, not punish those that need the most help. Third, by understanding that education is about teaching and learning and that we need quality teachers in our classrooms. John Kerry and John Edwards have a plan that will recruit and retain more quality teachers, pay them more, and ask for more accountability in return. Fourth, helping more Americans afford college through service payback programs. Fifth, increasing availability of higher levels of mathematics and science courses, access to higher education, and lifelong learning opportunities for all Americans. For John Kerry and John Edwards , this isn't empty rhetoric. They've taken action. They both supported more funding in exchange for more accountability. They both opposed private school vouchers. And they will work to increase more choice within the public schools. John Kerry successfully created the national youthbuild program for at-risk youth, promoted computer training for teachers, and gained national recognition for the school improvement act. I want all children in America to have their hopes restored so that they can dream their dreams and live their dreams. John Kerry and John Edwards will give them that hope and assure America a strong and bright future. Let's Elect Kerry and Edwards. Thank you.

speaker: Wellington Webb
text: Thank you Governor Richardson. My friend and outstanding public servant and a great American leader delegates, alternates and Colorado delegates, my home state, and honored guests, it is my privilege to introduce one of the future leaders of our party, the President of the college Democrats of America, Ashley Bell.

speaker: Alexandra Kerry
text: Thank you. Thank you, Vanessa. Well, it is an incredible experience to be here tonight. And I have to admit that it hasn't been easy to sift through years of memories about my father and find those few that might best tell you who John Kerry Reallys. So let me just begin with one July day when jean-sebastien -- Vanessa and I were kids. It's a silly story but true and it's one of my favorite memories about my father. We were standing on a dock, waiting for a boat to take us on a summer trip. Vanessa, the scientist, had passed -- packed all of her animals, including her favorite hamster. Our overzealous golden retriever got tangled in the leash and knocked the cage off the dock. Licorice, the unlucky hamster as he became known, bubbled down to a watery doom. That might have been the end of the story. A mock funeral at sea and tears for a hamster lost. But my dad jumped in, grabbed an oar, fished the cage from the water, hunched over the soggy hamster and began to add minute Sterc.p.r.! -- at minister c.p.r. ! Now, there are are still to this day some reports of mouth-to-mouth, but I admit it's probably a trick of memory. The hamster was never quite right after that, but he lived. Now, like I said, it may sound silly and we still laugh about it today, but it was serious to us, and that's what mattered to my father. Years later when I was driving back to college to him brooding as only a 19-year-old can, my father told me to look outside the car. He said ally, this is a beautiful day. Feel the sun. Look at the country you live in. The passion of his words makes me remember them still 10 years later. He said to me, "I know men your exact age who thought they had the same future you have, whose families were never born, who never again walked on American soil. They don't feel the sun. " ally, he said, "if there's something you don't like, something that needs to be done, change it, but never, ever give up. " remember, he said, remember that you are alive and that you are an American. Those two things make you the luckiest little girl in the world. Even now I look back at that and I think about what my dad has been through in his life because he's quiet about those things. My sister and I had to sneak upstairs when we were kids and read his letters from Vietnam. Who knew a 23-year-old could have seen so much so young? To every little girl her father is a hero. And it's taken some getting used to that my father actually is one. And it's not just in the obvious ways. Because he likes to listen as much as he likes to talk, because he's studious in the way someone is when everything in the whole world interests him, because he leads by example, and because he trusts people with the truth and doesn't play to our baser instincts. And let me tell you this -- thank you -- let me tell you this. When he loves you as he loves me and my sister and his family, as he loves the men who fought beside him, there is no sacrifice too great and when he cares for you as he cares for this country, there are no surer hands and no wiser heart. So when he teaches you by the lights he has led as he taught me and my sister all of our lives, there is no better lesson, that the future of this country is not only his life's work, it's mine and yours and it's all of ours. And if we want our children to Breathe Clean Air and Drink Clean Water, if we want them to control their own bodies, if we want them -- if we want them to protect the liberties and opportunities that are our birthrights, we must be involved in the struggle because on that day in that car my father was right, we are the luckiest people in the world. We walk on this soil. We feel this sun. And we are Americans. And now, it is our great pleasure and it is our great, great pride to introduce our father, John Kerry.

speaker: Andre Heinz
text: Good evening. In 1995, John Kerry became part of my life when he married my mother. And it didn't me very long to see that he is an incredible man. I have seen him as a husband, a father, a public servant, a sportsman, and a stepfather one could only dream of. He always sees me as a person that I am. What does it say about someone who is able to be all those things? What must he possess to excel at every one of them? He must be loving. He must be devoted. He must be humble, compassionate, decisive, and a peace-maker, especially if you know my brothers and sisters. When John Kerry came into our lives, we knew we would be stronger at home because he commands respect and he knows how to give it. This is a John Kerry I know, my stepfather, my friend, and the next the President of the United States! Just kidding, it's only Chris. My brother and I now have the pleasure to introduce two remarkable people we've grown to know and love and admire over these last nine years, our two sisters, Alex and Vanessa Kerry.

speaker: Anna Burger
text: Hello. I'm Anna Berger. Secretary Treasurer of service employees international union and a delegate from Pennsylvania. And I'm here today with Terry Murphy, a nurse and an seiu member from dubuque, Iowa. As the largest union of health care workers in this country, seiu is working every day to make sure that John Kerry is our next president. Our 1. 6 million members are on the front lines of the health care crisis and they've lived with four years of policies that have made it worse and are hurting working families. 44 million Americans go without health care every single day. As many as the total populations of California, New York, and Texas combined. And millions more have insurance plans that offer little or no coverage. Nurses like terry and other health care voters are speaking out and following candidates on the campaign trail to make sure that they understand the crisis, and today we have more than 250,000 others who have joined in demanding a resolution to the health care crisis. Last month, thousands of working families took to the streets and turned out in events in all 50 states to bridge the gap for quality, affordable health care for all. They crossed the bridge in San Francisco from the golden gate bridge all the way to the longfellow bridge in Boston, in Red States, and blue states, and every place in between and so tonight we are building on that enthusiasm so seiu is kicking off a campaign that will generate thousands of faxes to The White House calling on George Bush to offer a plan that will finally ensure every man, woman and child in America has health care that they need. But the truth is no amount of faxes will make this administration do the right thing. You too must become a health care voter. We need you to join this campaign. When John Kerry is elected President We will work from election day to inaugural day. We will hold town hall meetings across this country to build the grassroots support to ensure that his plan is passed by Congress. With your help, and John Kerry in the White House, together, we will create a health care plan and a health care system that puts people over profits. With your help, we will ensure that the first thing a doctor asks is where does it hurt? Instead of "where is your insurance card?" stand with us and make sure that the health care candidates win this November. Stand with us and we will send this administration packing. Stand with us and we will put John Kerry in the White House. Stand with us and we will win. Thank you very much.

speaker: Barbara Boxer
text: You are wonderful, California. Thank you, thank you so much. Thank you so much. Thanks to my family, thanks to California, thanks to all you wonderful delegates. I want to tell you something. Everywhere I go in California and across this great country, people tell me the same thing. They say this is the most important election of our lifetime. Let me tell you why they feel that way and why we all feel that way. It's because we know that the Kerry-Edwards team with their new leadership and the new leadership in Congress, America will be respected in the world again and the American dream will stop slipping into the fog of a distant memory. Well, I've been blessed with that dream. My mom was an immigrant who never graduated from high school because she had to work to support her widowed mother. My dad, he went to college at night, and my parents, they pushed and they struggled for their family. I went to good, free public schools. Kindergarten through college because of a uniquely American belief in opportunity for all. But today, today that belief is being tested. Too many workers can't make ends meet. Too many families can't afford health insurance or college tuition. Too many children go to schools that are overcrowded and they can't get into after school programs because they're short funded. I know we can do better and I know that you know we can do better. And why do I know we can do better? Because I believe in this country. I believe in our people, I believe in the American dream. And I believe in John Kerry and John Edwards. Yes. They understand that no worker should ever have to watch a good job being packed up and shipped overseas. They understand that a parent shouldn't have to worry every time their child slides into third base because there's no way to pay the doctor bill. They understand that our beloved grandparents shouldn't be boarding buses to other countries to buy life-saving drugs. And they understand that protecting our air and our water is about safeguarding the health of our families. They understand that in America we protect civil liberties and civil rights because then America wins. As John Kerry says, a strong America begins at home and with President Kerry we will be strong because our families will be strong. Our communities will be strong. Our economy will be strong. And the American dream will be strong. So here we are -- we've got Kerry, we've got Edwards, and now we need to get every American to understand what is at stake. It is up to us, the American people, nobody else but us. So God bless California. God bless the united states of America. Now let's get it done. Thank you very much, everybody. Thank you. Thank you.

speaker: Barney Frank
text: Thank you. Thank you. But if you clap later, it doesn't come out of my time. I want to begin on behalf of the national stone wall Democrats, the gay Lesbian, transgendered wing of The Democratic Party with an apology. I am sorry that the thought of two women who are in love seeking in Florida to sell elm news, that the Republicans decided to put aside the business of America, that they decided a couple of weeks ago that we couldn't deal with homeland security or a highway bill or education or health care, and they had to try and knock a big hole in the U.S.. Constitution. I guess I want to try to calm them down, so I'm going to come clean. You hear them talk about the gay agenda, and I'm going to be honest with you now, the fact is, we are who are gay, Lesbian, transgendered, we do have an agenda, and here it is. We think we should be able to fight for our country, like John Kerry, and serve in the military. We believe, revolutionary as it may sound to Jerry Falwell, we believe people out to be able to be hired for a job and be judged solely on how well they do the work and not over what somebody else thinks about who they are. We go so far as to believe that a 15-year-old who is different in a lot of ways sexually from others ought to be able to go to high school without getting beaten up. I admit it, we believe that. And we even believe, it's true, that when two people are in love and they are willing to be morally and legally committed to each other and financially responsible to each other that if they are prepared to get married, it's a good thing for the stability of society. We believe that. Now that's not all we believe. We also think that the marvelous health care that's been developed in America ought to be accessible to everybody, regardless of income. We're proud that America is the strongest nation in the world militarily and economically but we think that strength is enhanced, not diminished, when we cooperate with others. We think a strong environmental policy is good for our society and doesn't detract, and we believe that as we prosper and generate more wealth, if we share it in a fairer manner, if we do not simply let the rich get richer but worry about working people's ability to support themselves, America will benefit. Now, some of those things we believe because particularly we are gay and lesbians. To be honest with you, we don't know why we are, we just are. But we do know why we are Democrats. We know we are Democrats because it is The Democratic Party as opposed to our very right wing Republican opponents who support that agenda of allowing us to fight, of allowing us to marry, of allowing us to go forward as human beings with the rights of everyone else. And it is the Republican party that opposes them, and I add one last thing. With the differences between the two political parties on issues important to us as gay, Lesbian, and transgendered people so important, when Ralph Nader tells us that there is no significant difference between the parties, he trivializes our lives. Among the differences between the parties of overpowering significants are the differences that exist on the rights of people who are gay, Lesbian or transgendered to be treated fairly with the same rights as every other American, and so for these reasons I am proud on behalf of The National Federation of Stonewall Democrats to tell people how proudly we will vote in November for John Kerry and John Edwards. Thank you.

speaker: Bill Pascrell
text: I bring you greetings from the eighth congressional district of New Jersey. This week, we come together unified in our love for our country, determined in our mission to improve our future, inspired by the mab we will nominate tonight to be the next President of the United States. This week, represents the + this week, represents the commitment of our party and our noble mission -- Democrats must bring positive change for all Americans. Democrats must make our country safer, stronger, and more secure. We need a proven leader who has the experience, the knowledge, and the intellect to navigate the immense challenges of our century. We need John Kerry. His priorities are our priorities. He understands that the most basic responsibility of government is to protect family and protect country. John Kerry understands that tax cuts for our most fortunate citizens should never come at the expense of police officers on our streets. John Kerry knows that we shouldn't be building fire houses in Iraq while we're closing them in New York City. First responders should never be the last on the list of our priorities. The depth of John Kerry's life experiences help mold him into the leader that he is today, a leader that will never leave any first responder behind. Our police officers, firefighters, and emergency medical technicians desperately need more than pious rhetoric. John Kerry has a concrete plan that will deliver support for all public safety personnel. His plan to hire 100,000 new firefighters will better prepare towns and cities across America. His pledge to restore funding for the cops program will increase police presence in our most vulnerable areas. His plan to finally provide our uniformed personnel with communications equipment to coordinate with each other is absolutely vital from increased personnel to additional equipment, from advanced training to better coordination among our local, state, and federal agencies. John Kerry is focused on the right priorities. We need the strongest person to lead us. We will choose a decorated combat veteran, a tough-on-crime prosecutor, to make America secure again. I'm proud of John Kerry. I'm proud of our party. And I will be so proud of the great things we will accomplish once we win in November. My friends, we will no longer settle our -- we will no longer settle. Our time has come. Together, Americans will March forward with John Kerry to create a stronger, safer, and more optimistic America for generations to come. Thank you and God bless our great country. Thank you. God bless you.

speaker: Byron Dorgan
text: Fellow delegates, the late Congressman Claude pepper used to say that the constitution of our country, every even-numbered year, gives the American people the opportunity to grab the steering wheel and decide in which direction does America move. It is unique in the world that here on election day, all of the power exists in the power of one. The simple, elegant act of one voter casting one vote to affect America's destiny. And it is the power to make our country strong at home, respected abroad, the power to make the right decisions about protecting our national security, improving our schools, making health care affordable for all Americans, and keeping our promise to America's veterans. And this year it is the power of the voters to demand that we stop sending American jobs overseas. John Kerry and John Edwards know that we need to build an economy that keeps good jobs here at home with good benefits. No social program in America is more important than a good job that pays well, and this great political party has always stood for jobs and opportunity for all Americans. And I believe the voters will say this year they want an energy policy that does not hold us hostage to foreign oil. John Kerry and John Edwards know that we need to create a new energy future using hydrogen fuel cell vehicles and more renewable energy, a country that can put a man on the moon can surely create a new energy future and break our addiction to middle east oil. And I also believe that the voters will Elect A new administration willing to tackle the issues in America's heartland. Our country needs to stand up for the interests of family farmers and ranchers. For rural counties and small towns that are struggling all across America. Several decades ago when America's cities were suffering, our country created urban-renewal programs and model city programs to save our cities. Now, America needs to save our heartland. We need the tools to STEM the relentless depopulation and outmigration from rural areas and to build a new future in a part of America that is such an important part of our country. Our family farmers and ranchers don't ask for very much, just fair trade, fair prices, and an opportunity to make a decent living. Doing the right thing in rural America will make all of America stronger. We can do these things. We, the people, with the leadership of John Kerry and John Edwards, it is our moment now to grab the steering wheel of this great country, the greatest country on earth and steer toward a future of hope and opportunity for all Americans. Thank you. God bless you.

speaker: Carl Levin
text: Thank you, delegates, and thank you, Michigan. 229 years ago, just a few miles from here, a shot was fired that was heard around the world announcing a unique nation was about to be born. Tonight strong voice will be heatonight from this place a strong voice will be heard around the world proposing to change our nation's course. John Kerry understands that just as the current administration has squandered the budget surpluses of three years ago, so have their current go-it-alone foreign policies squandered the goodwill of much of the world and estranged us from our allies. John Kerry understands that to win the war against international terrorism, we must unite the world community against a common foe and not alienate the people of the world so that they sit on the sidelines of this struggle. John Kerry knows that America is at its strongest and best when we are respected not only for our military power but for the wisdom and restraint with which we use it. John Kerry knows that as the world's only superpower, we must be super wise in the ways that we use that power. John Kerry understands. That if we develop new nuclear weapons, if we walk away from treaties, if we ignore international law, other nations will follow that same dangerous course. John Kerry has felt the sting and the stress of combat. Our servicemen and women serving in Iraq and Afghanistan throughout the world will have in John Kerry a commander in chief for whom "leave no American behind" is not a slogan but a solemn personal duty, tested and proven under fire. We will have in John Kerry a commander in chief who has the competence and experience that our brave men and women in uniform deserve. As commander in chief, John Kerry will relentlessly pursue terrorists wherever they operate. But if there is no imminent threat to our nation, John Kerry will never send America's sons and daughters to fight and die in unilaterally declared wars. As we set a new course tonight, at home and abroad, we are guided by America's transcendant vision of a secure and peaceful world. We leave here confident that we have nominated the right captain to guide our beloved nation and a troubled world to calmer, safer Waters. And we depart focused and determined to do all in our power to put the helm of our ship of state in the steady hands of commander John Kerry. Thank you.

speaker: Corrine Brown
text: God is good. All the time, God is good. Greetings to my fellow Democrats and the wonderful people of the third congressional district of Florida. I am Corrine Brown. The same Corrine Brown that spoke out on the house of representatives about the travesty of the election of 2000. And whose words of protest was stricken from the congressional records. Understand me. In November of 2000, in my district alone, 27,000 votes was kicked out by faulty machines. Those votes went uncounted. 27,000 votes. In an area I represent that vote 98% Democratic. What's more, many voters was wrongly purged from registration lists for a variety of unacceptable reasons. I want to preserve for all people the right to vote and the right to have their votes counted. America, we have a lot of work to do. Much is at stake. We need leadership to help us educate our kids, uplift the middle class, and create jobs. Support our vete support our veterans. Provide affordable health care for families and seniors. I believe all Americans can count on John Kerry and John Edwards to do just that. A good reminder that the job lies ahead of us is to change the direction this country is going in is the dashboards on your car. R is for reverse and d is for drive. America, we must reverse and move into drive. The leadership team of John Kerry and John Edwards is a perfect vehicle to move us forward. What we do in the next 95 days will determine whether we go forward with John Kerry or stay in reverse. If we move forward, we need to shift into drive with two new drivers. John Kerry and Johnno Carrierringconnect 1200 wake up, America, so what happened in 2000 does not happen again in 2004. As I stake my -- as I take my seat, I want you to take a look at your hands. The future of America is in your hands. May God continue to bless America.

speaker: Ed Markey
text: Thank you ladies and gentlemen. My name is Ed Markey. And as someone who grew up across the mystic river in Massachusetts, I'm proud to stand before you today to help nominate my friend John Kerry to the highest office in the United States of America. President Bush once said that he wanted to be a uniter and not a divider. Well, congratulations, mr. President, you have united The Democratic Party in a way that we have not seen in a generation. Tonight we Democrats are reaching out not just to our own party but Republicans and independent voters who are asking some very basic questions. Are we as strong as we can be at home? Do countries respect us abroad? Are we making the right choices about spending precious dollars on war, on homeland security, on domestic needs? For more and more Americans the answer to these questions is a resounding no. So we're gathered here to say yes to a stronger, safer America, an America that gains friends instead of enemies, an American that creates jobs instead of sending jobs offshore, an American that invests in the future instead of saddling our children with crippling debt. It's time to give our hospitals, our nursing homes, our seniors the resources they need to cope with diseases such as alzheimer's, diabetes and parkinson's. When hope for the sick is at stake, it is time for a new leader. We must clean up our environment. Smog in the air is becoming asthma in the lungs of our children and elderly. When clean air and clean water are at stake, it is time for a new leader. We must stop nickel and diming our homeland security heros, our firefighters and our local police. We need to protect our ports, our chemical plants and nuclear facilities when the safety of our homeland is at stake. It is time for a new leader. Instead of thumbing our noses at treaties and abandoning international cooperation with the doctrine of leap before you look preemption, it is time to return America to the community of nations. If we want fewer nuclear weapons around the world, we must stop building new nuclear weapons here at home. When our credibility with our allies is at stake, it is time for a new leader. Two centuries ago, just a stone's throw from this podium, freedom-loving minutemen from every Massachusetts village and town gathered in Lexington and concord and with hope in their hearts they stood with their brave captain John Parker when he declared, "let it begin here. " are we ready to stand with our own captain John Kerry when tonight he declares, let it begin here? For 44 million uninsured Americans, let it begin here. For affordable prescription drugs for the elderly, let it begin here. For an energy future free from dependence on mideast oil, let it begin here. For protecting the civil rights of all Americans, let it begin here. For a country united by hope, not fear, let it begin here. Now is the time. This is the place. You are the people. And Kerry-Edwards is the ticket. Let it begin here. Thank you, all, so, so much.

speaker: Eleanor Holmes Norton
text: Delegates but they are different. Some like me were born in the nation's capital. My great grandfather was a runaway slave who came to Washington in the 18 50's. Others moved to the district of columbia every day for a different reason, because they want to live in the beautiful, livable capital of our nation, but be forewarned, fellow Americans, you will lose your voting representation in Congress and many citizenship rights if you decide to live in the capital of the free world. in the America of your nation's capital, we cannot spend our own local funds until Congress says so. And our local laws can be nullified against our Democratic will. Of course Uncle Sam counts on us to pay taxes every year, and our country can count on D.c. Residents to fight and die in the nation's wars as we always have since the first American died for freedom near here at Lexington. The people of the nation's capital in their quest for equal rights know who to count on to right this wrong. We can count on the party of Martin Luther King jr. And Fannie Lou haimer to be the party of equal rights and voting rights. We can count on John Kerry and John Edwards to lead the fight for equal voting representation in our Congress for the people of the district of columbia. One day the nation's capital will have equal rights to Democratic self-government as the 51st state. Until then, right now no American should be paying taxes without representation. Tonight, right now no Americans serving in Iraq should be denied voting representation in the Congress that sent them there. So tonight for the men and women from the district of columbia serving in our armed forces, for the 39 D.c. Delegates here and for the 600,000 Americans back home in D.c. , tonight we begin a journey to ensure that equal rights means everybody by electing John Kerry President of the United States of America.

speaker: Ellen Tauscher
text: Good afternoon. I'm representative Ellen tauscher from California's 10th congressional district, home of travis air force base where there are thousands of military personnel and their families doing their part to keep America strong. One of our nation's greatest challenges is to maintain a strong national defense. We need a fully equiped, expertly trained and well compensated military. We need a military sized to protect America and our allies with the capability to intervene where our interests may be threatened, and we need a President Who has the experience and the knowledge to use the military not because we want to but because we need to. As a member of our house armed services committee, I know that John Kerry will be that president. His brave service in the Navy gives him the experience to understand the challenges that confront a nation at war. He is committed to maintaining a military that can meet our needs today and is prepared for the challenges of the future. And his dedication to the men and women of America's armed forces is unwavering. John Kerry understands that we must make smart decisions about the size of our force. John Kerry will win the war and the peace in Iraq by equiping our troops properly and not stretching them too thin. Because we cannot continue to overburden our national guard and our reserve. These brave men and women are our community's first line of defense, serving at home, as police, firefighters and e.m.t. 's, we need them at home in our cities keeping us safe. John Kerry knows that our troops deserve the best equipment and supplies available. Every combat vehicle should be outfitted with the most advanced protective armor before it goes to hostile war zones. John Kerry knows that our military must be well paid for their hard work. Yearly, across the board pay raises are vital to troop morale and should continue to increase, and John Kerry knows that a healthy military is the first step to an expertly trained military. The Pentagon should administer quality and comprehensive health screenings prior to deployment. John Kerry has a plan for our armed services that strengthens our military and will set us upon the right path to a strong national defense. With John Kerry as President We will have a stronger and safer America. May God bless our troops. May God bless America. And may God bless all of you.

speaker: Fernando Ferrer
text: Thank you. Mr. Chairman, sister and brother Democrats, I am a proud Democrat, today and every day. I am also the son of New York City. My family came to New York from Puerto rico on the wings of hope and opportunity. We didn't learn about the American dream from reading it in a textbook, we learned it by living it, by working hard and playing by the rules. But today, that dream is in danger. Families work hard but the rules seem stacked against them. They don't understand why we build firehouses in Baghdad and shut them down in Brooklyn. We need a President Who will bring hope to every American family. We need a President Who will fight every day to expand opportunity for middle class families and those who want to work their way into the middle class just like mine did. We need President John Kerry because he believes. Because he believes we're a stronger nation when every family, when every child, has the same opportunities that I had. My friends, electing John Kerry is just as important today as electing John Kennedy was in 1960. Now, back then I was 10. Back then, I was 10. And every sunday, my family and I went to church and then along to my grandmother's ground-floor apartment on tinton avenue in the south Bronx for breakfast. As the neighbors walked by the window, they would ask my father, who had me joining him, who he was voting for and he would tell them, "Kennedy." I asked him, "why are we voting for Kennedy?" and he said, "because we're Democrats. " I asked him, "why are we Democrats?" and here's what he said. "because Democrats care about people like us." now, I was only 10. But I knew what he meant. And that, that moment, is when I became a Democrat. You see, Democrats care about people who work hard all week to spend some time with their families. About people living their lives and working hard just to do a little better for their kids. So this election, when the talk around the dinner table turns to politics, I expect my young grandson, Brendan, to ask me why I'm voting for John Kerry. I'll tell him, it's because I want for him the kind of America that made it possible for our own family to achieve the dream and promise of America. An America where no one puts him in harm's way without a good reason. An America where no one is left behind. Our America, again. Now, I've noticed that our President Speaks a bit of spanish. He must be trying to reach out. Well, I have a message for our President in the language he professes to understand. Senor presidente. Mr. President, your policies are bad in any language. Thank you. God bless you.

speaker: Francis Slay
text: Good afternoon, Democrats. My name is Francis slay. I'm the Mayor Of St. Louis, Missouri. The home of the world's largest brewery. The stunning gateway arch. The national league leading St. Louis cardinals. And St. Louis' favorite son, Congressman Dick Gephardt. To remain strong at home, we need a President Who knows what is important. We must be safe at home. That means cutting the red tape and making sure our first responders in cities and counties have the resources they need to fight the war on terror here. Being safe at home also means keeping our families safe from crime. In St. Louis, we've seen an unprecedented reduction in crime. Our city is safer, much safer, but those gains are being threatened by cuts in the cops program and in the law enforcement block grant program. We must also invest our money in our children. By giving them a quality education so that they will be good citizens and have a chance to make their dreams come true. During the last year in St. Louis, we took the unusual step of hiring a national turnaround firm to reorganize and to make our school district much better. They streamlined the administration to focus more resources in the classroom, and yet children will continue to be left behind if the federal government fails to meet its obligations. John Kerry and John Edwards will not rest until every child has a chance for a good education. The working poor will continue to fall further behind. Wages are not keeping up with the rising costs of raising a family. And health care continues to be too expensive. John Kerry and John Edwards have a real economic plan that creates good opportunities for all Americans and strengthens the middle class. I don't want or expect the federal government to do our jobs. We're doing them. But the federal government takes a lot of our hard-earned tax dollars. We need a President Who knows what's important when he invests that money. I want what John Kerry promises. A government whose priority is its children, and strong, healthy families. Thank you so much.

speaker: Gary Locke
text: Good evening. From the great state of Washington, from a delegation of energized and committed delegates for the election of John Kerry and John Edwards as President And Vice President of the United States of America, hello, Democrats. I stand before you tonight as a child of combraunts. Like so many of you, our family came to America in search of freedom, opportunity, and a better life. My father joined The United States army during world war II and was part of the normandy invasion landing at Utah Beach but actually my grandfather came to America 100 years ago and served as a house boy for a family in the state capital in exchange for english lessons. And now 100 years later I serve as the Governor Of the great state of Washington, living in the governor's residence with my beautiful wife Mona, our two lovely children emily and dylan. Just one mile from the house where my grandfather swept floors, washed dishes, and cooked meals. Only in America. And I learned english when I was in kindergarten, the same time that my mom was learning english to become a United States citizen, and I remember the day that she was sworn in. As a naturalized citizen on the fourth of July. Only in America. Through hard work and education, our family's dreams were rewarded. We found an America where reaching the middle class was within everyone's grasp and every generation could go farther than the generation before. We found a land respected and admired around the world. That's the real America. That's the real America our families came in search of and aspired to. And that's the real America we will restore and reclaim when we Elect President John Kerry and Vice President John Edwards. John Kerry and John Edwards will build an America that is stronger at home and respected around the world. They will create good-paying middle class jobs. And John Kerry and John Edwards will make sure that every American has affordable, quality health care. John Kerry and John Edwards understand that to be truly independent as a nation we must end our reliance on middle eastern oil, and they know that for America to effectively lead the world, to end tyranny and conflict and human rights abuses in other nations, we, our leaders, must be respected around the world. They have the experience, the commitment, and the credibility to make America's foreign policy one of inclusion, not exclusion. A safer America is not an America standing alone. It's an America standing with our allies, standing inside, not outside the family of nations. America has the technology, the inventiveness and the spirit to realize the dreams and the aspirations of every American family. But what we need is leadership and vision. And John Kerry and John Edwards are those leaders and they have that vision. Our parents and grandparents and ancestors came to these shores in search of freedom, opportunity, and a better life. During world war II, why did African-American men living in segregated communities here in America fight in europe and in the pacific and form the famed tuskegee errmen? Why did Japanese Americans form the 100th infantry battalion and the combat team, the most famed decorated unit in American military history, while their moms and dads and their younger brothers and sisters lived behind armed concentration camps? Why? Because they believed in the essential goodness and the destiny of America. Together working with the American people, John Kerry and John Edwards will restore those values, restore those dreams, and restore the real America we all love and admire. God bless you all and God bless America.

speaker: Harvey Gantt
text: Thank you. Fellow Democrats, ladies and gentlemen, I'm Harvey Gantt and I'm from North Carolina. And I stand here humbled, honored, and proud to place a nomination for the office of Vice President of the United States of America my friend and my Senator Center the great state of North Carolina, John Edwards. I'm so pleased and I'm sure most of you delegates feel the same that our nominee and next president, John Kerry, had the wisdom, foresight, and courage to tell this convention and the nation that he wanted John Edwards as his running mate. Now I've only known John for about 10 years, and yet we share so much. We were both born in South Carolina. Before we moved north, which for Us was North Carolina. We have both been activists and eyewitnesses to marked social changes in our region. We've grown up, we've raised our families and educated our children in a region where change, both social, economic, and racial has been palpable and easy to see. John Edwards comes from working folk, just like me. John Edwards worked hard and excelled to get his education. Just like me. When you're raised as John Edwards was, in a small town, like robbins, North Carolina, you get to understand poverty and unemployment or inquath health care firsthand, by seeing the daily struggles of your friends and your neighbors. This is a man who knows from whence he has come. He knows education can make a difference in people's lives. He knows a decent job paying a livable wage can help to build strong families. He knows that the some very nicey of Medicare and social security for our seniors may be the difference between a retirement of worry and one of dignity. No, I haven't known John Edwards my whole life, but the lives we've lived and the values we share have taught me that you can be a soul mate before you meet. He cares about building a more united America. One America. His vision is an America devoid of the lines between the haves and the have nots. His vision is an America where race is an honest dialogue in our community, where we seek to heal the racial divisions that keep holding Us back from becoming the America we need to be. in the 1990's, I ran two Senate campaigns in North Carolina that many of you may remember. My opponent ran a campaign of divisiveness. I firmly believe that an appeal to our best instincts as citizens, harnessing our hopes and our aspirations could free Us from the perils of poverty and ignorance and bigotry. The fact that my state chose as its Senator John Edwards has vindicated the beliefs I held in those earlier campaigns. His positive campaigns, his optimistic outlook and his can-do spirit have infused Us all and invigorated Us all. He appeals to our best hopes, not our worst fears. John Edwards makes Us all proud to be Americans. And those of Us from North Carolina are even more proud to claim him. For he represents the best that we can offer America, at a time when strong, positive leadership is needed. America, you will get a vice-presidential candidate whose values fit hand in glove with John Kerry. America, you will get a man who will wake up every morning thinking about how he can help President Kerry bring the promise of America home to working families. America's families will get a man who will advocate for their values, for their hopes, and their aspirations to succeed. He is a father, he is a friend, he is a leader, and he is my senator. Mr. Chairman, fellow Democrats, ladies and gentlemen, I am pleased, as a friend and proud as a north carolinian to nominate John Edwards as our next Vice President of these United States. Thank you.

speaker: Jack Ford
text: Hello, Democrats. Let me give a special hello to my fellow ohioans. I bring you greetings from Toledo, Ohio, home of the Toledo mudhens and the world famous jeep. We ohioans know something about electing presidents. No Republican President In recent history has won an election without Ohio. We know what that means for Ohio Democrats. We must, and we will do everything possible to win the election, and we will win Ohio. As Mayor Of the great city of Toledo, I have seen firsthand the impact of the last four years that we have had in our cities and in our working families. In Toledo and all across America, middle-class families are being squeezed. More than anyone else, working families are shouldering the burden of spiraling health care premiums. Higher gas prices. Rising child care expenses. But wages that don't keep up with what it takes to make ends meet. The middle class and those families that strive to make it to the middle class don't see the light at the end of the tunnel. But let me assure you, brighter days are headed for America. When we Elect John Kerry and John Edwards, you and I must be unrelenting, every single day, to make this happen. Victory depends on the fight we wage town to town, ward to ward, precinct to precinct, door to door, hand to hand and as someone said to me last night, eyeball to eyeball, because in John Kerry and in John Edwards, we have a team that has a plan to make America stronger at home and more respected in the world. They will revive both Ohio's and America's manufacturing sector. Create new jobs, and protect existing ones, they will work to stop the unconscionable practice of exporting American jobs overseas. And they will push fair trade practices that help American workers, not hurt them. Health care will no longer be a privilege. But it will be a right. And urban America will finally get a sound housing policy, a common-sense approach to fixing our economy, and an administration that works with us, not pushing against us. Now, more than ever, we need a President Who stands for America and for working families. Now, more than ever, we need a President Who understands how to get the economy running and to bring back jobs to our communities. Fellow Democrats, the time for talk is almost over. The hour of actions upon us. And tonight, the man and the hour will meet. And that man will be the next President of the United States, John Kerry. Thank you.

speaker: Jack Reed
text: My fellow Democrats, my fellow Americans, at this moment of great challenge abroad and home we need the leadership of John Kerry, leadership tested in the jungles of Vietnam as a swift boat commander, leadership tempered through years of dedicated service in the United States Senate, leadership attuned to the needs to support and strengthen our armed forces and our intelligence agencies while building bridges to our allies throughout the world. John Kerry will ensure that our fighting men and women have all the resources they need from body armor to armored vehicles, to carry out their missions effectively and safely. John Kerry has a plan to increase the size of the army so that our military is not stretched too thin and a plan so that our national guard's men and women are not called away from their family and jobs for more time than needed but John Kerry also understands that national security relies not just on a strong military. He plans to strengthen our defenses against our enemies with an ambitious and aggressive, diplomatic and intelligence plan. John Kerry will work with our allies and earn back the world's trust so that America once again leads with strong alliances so that our nation is not left exposed and alone. He will transform our intelligence services by maximizing coordination among our intelligence agencies and making sure that we have the personnel at home and abroad to get the job done. And he will embrace the recommendations of the 9/11 commission to prevent another terrorist attack in this country. We face great challenges around the world and here at home but John Kerry has faced great challenges before. Under his leadership and with his wisdom and courage, we will prevail. Thank you very much.

speaker: James Clyburn
text: Thank you. Thank you very much. Fellow delegates, alternates, honored guests, my father, a fundamentalist minister, taught me and my two brothers lessons in very memorable ways. One day he happened upon the three of us as we were engaged in a disagreement that had turned physical. He handed each of us a piece of cord string and challenged us to pop it. We each tried valiantly and failed. Dad then took that piece of cord string and began to rub it in the palms of his hands. Rather quickly the friction his rubbing created unraveled that string. He handed each one of us a thread and at his request we easily popped them. With this illustration my father explained the harmful consequences of allowing little disagreements among us to cause too much friction and separate us. I often tell that story but it seems to have more meaning and in much broader terms today than ever before. There are many threads woven together that create the fabric of America. This beautiful tapestry has begun to fray and weaken because unsound policies and unfair practices are creating friction among us at home and between us and our friends around the world. America needs to be set on a different course. In four years we have seen record budget surpluses turn into record-setting deficit. In four years we have seen an economy that was averaging nearly three million new jobs a year turn into one that lost nearly two million private sector jobs over the last three years. Americans are crying out for a new vision from our leaders and a more responsible government. They want to restore good relationships with our friends and allies around the world. John Kerry and John Edwards will give us just that. A carry-Edwards administration will repair the thread -- a Kerry-Edwards administration will remare the thread bare holes growing in our nation's fabric. The Kerry-Edwards health care plan will add strength and security to America's families. The Kerry-Edwards education plan will adequately fund public education and make postsecondary education and training affordable for all our nation's youth. Kerry-Edwards will close tax loopholes that encourage sending jobs overseas and implement tax policies that will reward companies that create new jobs here at home. Kerry-Edwards will end unfair tax breaks for millionaires and cut 98% of Americans' taxes. I know the compassion and values of John Kerry and John Edwards. John Edwards and I were born and reared in the same neck of the woods as we call it down in north and South Carolina. I grew up in a home where the recitation of a bible verse was required every morning at breakfast. I was taught that if you make a dollar, you should save a nickel. I was taught that when you leave the room, you turn out the lights to conserve energy. These were lessons of faith and conservatism. But on sunday mornings when dad called for the weekly official, he always asked the congregations to give liberally. So I learned at an early age that in some things we should be conservative and in others we should be liberal. Those values instilled in me are the same values shared by jerk -- by John Kerry and John Edwards. Each of us represents a different thread in the fabric of our nation. If it's worn in someplaces, a little shabby in others but still a unified cord. The Kerry-Edwards team will rekindle hope in our people and repair America's relationships around the world. They will reweave our frayed frab rick into a vibrant -- frab rick -- fabric into a vibrant tab industry that will create a better America -- tapestry, that will create a better America, stronger at home and respected around the world. God bless each and every one of you. God bless these united states of America.

speaker: James Socas
text: Thank you fellow Democrats and fellow Americans. Special greetings to the delegation from my home state, the great commonwealth of Virginia. My friends, my friends, this year we are going to make history. And deliver our commonwealth for John Kerry and John Edwards. But we're not doing it just for them. We're doing it for Thomas Jefferson and George Washington and James Madison and all the other Virginians and patriots who founded our great nation. For this country is headed in the opposite direction, of all the principles they stood for and fought for and died for. So mark this election of 2004. This is the year we turn things around. This is the year Virginians will lead again. It's time to return to a principle. We've forgotten for four years one at the heart of our country, and our state. The principle of a commonwealth. The idea, the idea that we are all in this together with common responsibilities and a shared destiny. Young and old, rich and poor, black and white. But This White House, This White House thinks Americans are consumers, not citizens. Prisoners of our pocketbooks, tied only to our tax cuts. They sell us short. Every day, every day across our lands, Americans are giving to causes greater than themselves. And today, today in foreign lands our soldiers are fighting for our country.
